addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content: 1 # Goloo 2 3 4 5 Disclaimer: This works for me — that's the entire guarantee. Built with AI in the loop, so check your own biases before you love it or hate it on principle. Use at your own risk, fork freely, and don't @ me when it explodes. (But do drop me a note if it helps — pay it forward.) 6 7 Provision identical servers locally or in the cloud from a single config file. 8 9 ## The Problem 10 11 You want to build a server. You need it running locally for development, then deploy the same thing to AWS or DigitalOcean for production. Normally this means maintaining two or three separate setups — Vagrant/Multipass configs for local, Terraform/CloudFormation for cloud — that inevitably drift apart. 12 13 Goloo uses one JSON config and one cloud-init YAML to create the same server anywhere. 14 15 ## About the Name 16 17 **Go** + **Leeloo**. Named after the character from The Fifth Element who carries a "multipass" — the same Multipass that Canonical named their VM manager after. Goloo extends that idea: one tool, one config, anywhere you need to go. See [docs/about-the-name.md](docs/about-the-name.md) for the full story. 18 19 ## Quick Start 20 21 ### 1. Create a config folder 22 23 Each VM config lives in its own folder under `~/.config/goloo/stacks/`. The folder contains a `config.json` and an optional `cloud-init.yaml`. 24 25 Create `~/.config/goloo/stacks/web-server/config.json`: 26 27 ```json 28 { 29 "vm": { 30 "name": "web-server", 31 "cpus": 2, 32 "memory": "2G", 33 "disk": "20G", 34 "image": "24.04", 35 "users": [ 36 {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} 37 ] 38 } 39 } 40 ``` 41 42 ### 2. Write a cloud-init file 43 44 This is a standard [cloud-init](https://cloudinit.readthedocs.io/) YAML that defines what your server looks like. Goloo fetches SSH keys from GitHub automatically using the `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` placeholder. 45 46 Create `~/.config/goloo/stacks/web-server/cloud-init.yaml`: 47 48 ```yaml 49 #cloud-config 50 users: 51 - name: ubuntu 52 sudo: ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL 53 shell: /bin/bash 54 ssh_authorized_keys: 55 - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY} 56 57 package_update: true 58 package_upgrade: true 59 60 packages: 61 - nginx 62 - certbot 63 - python3-certbot-nginx 64 - fail2ban 65 - ufw 66 67 runcmd: 68 - ufw allow OpenSSH 69 - ufw allow 'Nginx Full' 70 - ufw --force enable 71 - systemctl enable nginx 72 - systemctl start nginx 73 ``` 74 75 ### 3. Create the server locally 76 77 ```bash 78 goloo create web-server 79 ``` 80 81 This launches a Multipass VM with nginx, certbot, and fail2ban installed. SSH in with: 82 83 ```bash 84 goloo ssh web-server 85 ``` 86 87 ### 4. Deploy the same server to AWS 88 89 Add AWS-specific fields to `config.json`: 90 91 ```json 92 { 93 "vm": { 94 "name": "web-server", 95 "instance_type": "t3.small", 96 "os": "ubuntu-24.04", 97 "region": "us-east-1", 98 "users": [ 99 {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} 100 ] 101 }, 102 "dns": { 103 "hostname": "web", 104 "domain": "example.com", 105 "ttl": 300 106 } 107 } 108 ``` 109 110 Then create the instance: 111 112 ```bash 113 goloo create web-server --aws 114 ``` 115 116 Goloo creates a CloudFormation stack with an EC2 instance, security group, and (if a `dns` section is present) a Route53 A record pointing `web.example.com` at the instance IP. 117 118 See `examples/aws-web-server/` for a complete config with all AWS parameters. 119 120 ### 5. Deploy the same server to DigitalOcean 121 122 Add DigitalOcean-specific fields to `config.json`: 123 124 ```json 125 { 126 "vm": { 127 "name": "web-server", 128 "do_size": "s-2vcpu-4gb", 129 "do_region": "nyc3", 130 "do_image": "ubuntu-24-04-x64", 131 "users": [ 132 {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} 133 ] 134 }, 135 "dns": { 136 "hostname": "web", 137 "domain": "example.com", 138 "ttl": 300 139 } 140 } 141 ``` 142 143 Export your DigitalOcean API token and create the droplet: 144 145 ```bash 146 export DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN=your_token_here 147 goloo create web-server --do 148 ``` 149 150 Goloo creates a Droplet, attaches a firewall, and (if a `dns` section is present) creates DNS A records in your DigitalOcean domain. 151 152 See `examples/do-server/` for a complete DigitalOcean config. 153 154 ## Example: Building a Development Server 155 156 A more complete example — a polyglot dev server with Docker, Go, Node.js, and Python: 157 158 `~/.config/goloo/stacks/dev/config.json`: 159 160 ```json 161 { 162 "vm": { 163 "name": "dev", 164 "cpus": 4, 165 "memory": "4G", 166 "disk": "40G", 167 "image": "24.04", 168 "instance_type": "t3.medium", 169 "do_size": "s-4vcpu-8gb", 170 "do_region": "nyc3", 171 "do_image": "ubuntu-24-04-x64", 172 "users": [ 173 {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} 174 ] 175 } 176 } 177 ``` 178 179 Several cloud-init templates are included in `configs/` for common setups. Copy one to `~/.config/goloo/stacks/dev/cloud-init.yaml`: 180 181 | Config | What it installs | 182 |--------|-----------------| 183 | `configs/base.yaml` | curl, wget, vim, htop, git | 184 | `configs/dev.yaml` | build-essential, ripgrep, tmux, fzf, jq | 185 | `configs/python-dev.yaml` | Python 3, pip, venv, uv | 186 | `configs/go-dev.yaml` | Go 1.23, gopls, delve debugger | 187 | `configs/node-dev.yaml` | Node.js 22, npm, pnpm, yarn | 188 | `configs/claude-dev.yaml` | All of the above plus Docker | 189 190 Develop locally: 191 192 ```bash 193 goloo create dev 194 goloo ssh dev 195 ``` 196 197 When it's ready for the cloud: 198 199 ```bash 200 goloo create dev --aws # AWS EC2 201 goloo create dev --do # DigitalOcean Droplet 202 ``` 203 204 ## Commands 205 206 ``` 207 goloo create Create a local VM (Multipass) 208 goloo create --aws Create an AWS EC2 instance 209 goloo create --do Create a DigitalOcean Droplet 210 goloo delete Delete VM (auto-detects provider) 211 goloo list List local VMs 212 goloo list --aws List AWS VMs 213 goloo list --do List DigitalOcean Droplets 214 goloo ssh SSH into VM 215 goloo status Show VM status 216 goloo stop Stop VM 217 goloo start Start VM 218 goloo dns swap Update DNS records to current VM IP (AWS and DigitalOcean) 219 ``` 220 221 ### Flags 222 223 | Flag | Description | 224 |------|-------------| 225 | `--aws` | Use AWS provider | 226 | `--do`, `--digitalocean` | Use DigitalOcean provider | 227 | `--local` | Use local Multipass provider | 228 | `--dir PATH` | Goloo config directory (default: `~/.config/goloo`); stacks at `/stacks/` | 229 | `--folder`, `-f PATH` | Full path to stacks folder (overrides `--dir`) | 230 | `--users`, `-u USERS` | GitHub usernames for SSH key injection (comma-separated) | 231 | `--verbose`, `-v` | Show detailed progress | 232 | `--version` | Show version | 233 | `--help`, `-h` | Show help | 234 235 Flags can go in any order: 236 237 ```bash 238 goloo create web-server --aws 239 goloo create --do web-server -f ~/my-servers 240 goloo create web-server -u gherlein 241 goloo create web-server -u "alice,bob" 242 ``` 243 244 ### The `--users` flag 245 246 The `--users`/`-u` flag provides GitHub usernames whose SSH public keys are fetched and injected into the cloud-init template. This overrides any users defined in the config JSON. 247 248 ```bash 249 goloo create web-server -f ./examples/ -u gherlein 250 ``` 251 252 The first username maps to the `ubuntu` VM user (the default for Multipass and most cloud-init templates). Additional usernames become both the VM user and the GitHub lookup: 253 254 ```bash 255 goloo create web-server -u "alice,deploy-bot" 256 # alice → VM user "ubuntu", SSH keys from github.com/alice.keys 257 # deploy-bot → VM user "deploy-bot", SSH keys from github.com/deploy-bot.keys 258 ``` 259 260 ### Environment Variables 261 262 | Variable | Description | 263 |----------|-------------| 264 | `GOLOO_STACK_FOLDER` | Default base folder for configs (overridden by `--folder`/`-f`) | 265 | `DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN` | DigitalOcean API token (required when using `--do`) | 266 267 Precedence for stack folder: `--folder`/`-f` > `--dir` > `GOLOO_STACK_FOLDER` > `~/.config/goloo/stacks/` 268 269 `DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN` is read at runtime and never written to disk or logged. 270 271 ### Provider Auto-Detection 272 273 When you don't pass `--aws`, `--do`, or `--local`, goloo detects the provider from saved state: 274 275 1. Config has a `do` state section (previously created with `--do`) → DigitalOcean 276 2. Config has an `aws` state section (previously created with `--aws`) → AWS 277 3. Otherwise → Multipass 278 279 A config can include a `dns` section without triggering a cloud provider — DNS records are only created when you explicitly pass `--aws` or `--do`. This means `goloo delete web-server` does the right thing regardless of where the VM was created. 280 281 ### Legacy Flags 282 283 For users migrating from [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2): 284 285 ```bash 286 goloo -c -n web-server # Same as: goloo create web-server --aws 287 goloo -d -n web-server # Same as: goloo delete web-server --aws 288 ``` 289 290 ## Config File 291 292 Each VM's configuration lives in a named folder under a base directory (default `~/.config/goloo/stacks/`): 293 294 ``` 295 ~/.config/goloo/stacks/ 296 └── web-server/ 297 ├── config.json 298 └── cloud-init.yaml 299 ``` 300 301 The `config.json` file has two types of sections: **input sections** that you write by hand, and **state sections** that goloo manages automatically. 302 303 ### Input vs State 304 305 | Section | Type | Who writes it | When | 306 |---------|------|---------------|------| 307 | `vm` | Input | You | Before first `goloo create` | 308 | `dns` | Input | You | Before first `goloo create` | 309 | `local` | State | Goloo | Written by `goloo create`, removed by `goloo delete` | 310 | `aws` | State | Goloo | Written by `goloo create --aws`, removed by `goloo delete` | 311 | `do` | State | Goloo | Written by `goloo create --do`, removed by `goloo delete` | 312 313 Input sections (`vm`, `dns`) are never modified by goloo. They define what you want. State sections (`local`, `aws`, `do`) track what goloo created, so it knows how to manage and clean up resources later. 314 315 Because input and state are separate, the same config supports simultaneous local and cloud deployments. Creating a local VM writes a `local` section; creating an AWS instance writes an `aws` section; creating a DigitalOcean Droplet writes a `do` section. Deleting one removes only its state section without touching the others. 316 317 ### Minimal config 318 319 A config file needs only a `vm` section with a name and at least one user: 320 321 ```json 322 { 323 "vm": { 324 "name": "devbox", 325 "users": [ 326 {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} 327 ] 328 } 329 } 330 ``` 331 332 Everything else has defaults. This creates a local VM with 2 CPUs, 2G RAM, 20G disk, Ubuntu 24.04. 333 334 ### Full config example 335 336 This shows all input fields across all providers: 337 338 ```json 339 { 340 "vm": { 341 "name": "web-server", 342 "cpus": 4, 343 "memory": "4G", 344 "disk": "40G", 345 "image": "24.04", 346 "instance_type": "t3.small", 347 "os": "ubuntu-24.04", 348 "region": "us-east-1", 349 "vpc_id": "", 350 "subnet_id": "", 351 "do_size": "s-2vcpu-4gb", 352 "do_region": "nyc3", 353 "do_image": "ubuntu-24-04-x64", 354 "do_vpc_id": "", 355 "ssh_cidr": "0.0.0.0/0", 356 "web_cidr": "0.0.0.0/0", 357 "mounts": [ 358 {"source": "/home/user/code", "target": "/home/ubuntu/code"} 359 ], 360 "users": [ 361 {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "alice"}, 362 {"username": "deploy", "github_username": "deploy-bot"} 363 ] 364 }, 365 "dns": { 366 "hostname": "web", 367 "domain": "example.com", 368 "ttl": 300, 369 "zone_id": "", 370 "is_apex_domain": true, 371 "cname_aliases": ["www"] 372 } 373 } 374 ``` 375 376 Fields are provider-specific — each provider ignores fields it doesn't use (see the reference tables below). 377 378 ### After creating a local VM 379 380 After `goloo create web-server`, goloo adds a `local` section: 381 382 ```json 383 { 384 "vm": { ... }, 385 "local": { 386 "ip": "192.168.64.5" 387 } 388 } 389 ``` 390 391 After `goloo delete web-server`, the `local` section is removed entirely. 392 393 ### After creating an AWS instance 394 395 After `goloo create web-server --aws`, goloo adds an `aws` section with all the resources it provisioned: 396 397 ```json 398 { 399 "vm": { ... }, 400 "dns": { ... }, 401 "aws": { 402 "public_ip": "54.1.2.3", 403 "instance_id": "i-0123456789abcdef0", 404 "stack_id": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456:stack/goloo-web-server/abc", 405 "stack_name": "goloo-web-server", 406 "security_group": "sg-abc123", 407 "ami_id": "ami-0123456789abcdef0", 408 "vpc_id": "vpc-abc123", 409 "subnet_id": "subnet-def456", 410 "zone_id": "Z1234567890", 411 "fqdn": "web.example.com", 412 "dns_records": [ 413 {"name": "web.example.com", "type": "A", "value": "54.1.2.3", "ttl": 300}, 414 {"name": "example.com", "type": "A", "value": "54.1.2.3", "ttl": 300}, 415 {"name": "www.example.com", "type": "CNAME", "value": "web.example.com", "ttl": 300} 416 ] 417 } 418 } 419 ``` 420 421 After `goloo delete web-server`, the entire `aws` section is removed. The `vm` and `dns` input sections stay intact so you can re-create the server without editing the config. 422 423 If goloo created a VPC and subnet (because no default VPC existed), the `aws` section also tracks those resources so they are cleaned up on delete: 424 425 ```json 426 { 427 "aws": { 428 "created_vpc": true, 429 "created_subnet": true, 430 "internet_gateway_id": "igw-789", 431 "route_table_id": "rtb-abc", 432 "route_table_association_id": "rtbassoc-def", 433 ... 434 } 435 } 436 ``` 437 438 ### After creating a DigitalOcean Droplet 439 440 After `goloo create web-server --do`, goloo adds a `do` section: 441 442 ```json 443 { 444 "vm": { ... }, 445 "dns": { ... }, 446 "do": { 447 "droplet_id": 123456789, 448 "public_ip": "192.241.0.1", 449 "fqdn": "web.example.com", 450 "firewall_id": "bb4b2611-3d72-467b-8602-280330ecd65c", 451 "dns_records": [ 452 {"name": "web", "type": "A", "value": "192.241.0.1", "ttl": 300, "record_id": 12345}, 453 {"name": "@", "type": "A", "value": "192.241.0.1", "ttl": 300, "record_id": 12346}, 454 {"name": "www", "type": "CNAME", "value": "web.example.com.", "ttl": 300, "record_id": 12347} 455 ] 456 } 457 } 458 ``` 459 460 After `goloo delete web-server`, goloo deletes the Droplet, firewall, and DNS records, then removes the `do` section. The `vm` and `dns` input sections are preserved. 461 462 ### Simultaneous deployments 463 464 Because state is stored in separate sections, multiple providers can be active at once: 465 466 ```json 467 { 468 "vm": { ... }, 469 "dns": { ... }, 470 "local": { "ip": "192.168.64.5" }, 471 "aws": { "public_ip": "54.1.2.3", ... }, 472 "do": { "public_ip": "192.241.0.1", ... } 473 } 474 ``` 475 476 Delete each independently: 477 478 ```bash 479 goloo delete web-server --local # removes local section only 480 goloo delete web-server --aws # removes aws section only 481 goloo delete web-server --do # removes do section only 482 ``` 483 484 ### vm section reference 485 486 | Field | Default | Provider | Description | 487 |-------|---------|----------|-------------| 488 | `name` | (required) | All | VM name, used for stack naming and config lookup | 489 | `cpus` | 2 | Local | Number of CPUs | 490 | `memory` | `"2G"` | Local | RAM allocation | 491 | `disk` | `"20G"` | Local | Disk size | 492 | `image` | `"24.04"` | Local | Ubuntu version for Multipass | 493 | `mounts` | | Local | List of `{"source", "target"}` host directory mounts | 494 | `instance_type` | `"t3.micro"` | AWS | EC2 instance type | 495 | `os` | `"ubuntu-24.04"` | AWS | AMI lookup key | 496 | `region` | `"us-east-1"` | AWS | AWS region | 497 | `vpc_id` | | AWS | Specific VPC to use (auto-discovered if empty) | 498 | `subnet_id` | | AWS | Specific subnet to use (auto-discovered if empty) | 499 | `do_size` | | DigitalOcean | Droplet size slug (e.g., `"s-2vcpu-4gb"`) | 500 | `do_region` | | DigitalOcean | Region slug (e.g., `"nyc3"`, `"sfo3"`, `"lon1"`) | 501 | `do_image` | | DigitalOcean | Image slug (e.g., `"ubuntu-24-04-x64"`) | 502 | `do_vpc_id` | | DigitalOcean | VPC UUID to launch the Droplet into (optional) | 503 | `ssh_cidr` | `"0.0.0.0/0"` | DigitalOcean | CIDR allowed to reach port 22 on the Droplet firewall | 504 | `web_cidr` | `"0.0.0.0/0"` | DigitalOcean | CIDR allowed to reach ports 80 and 443 on the Droplet firewall | 505 | `users` | (required) | All | List of `{"username", "github_username"}` for SSH key injection | 506 507 ### dns section reference (AWS and DigitalOcean) 508 509 | Field | Default | Description | 510 |-------|---------|-------------| 511 | `hostname` | `vm.name` | Hostname portion of the FQDN | 512 | `domain` | | Hosted zone domain (e.g., `"example.com"`) | 513 | `ttl` | 300 | DNS record TTL in seconds | 514 | `zone_id` | | Route53 hosted zone ID (AWS only; auto-looked up from domain if empty) | 515 | `is_apex_domain` | `false` | Also create an A record at the zone apex (bare domain) | 516 | `cname_aliases` | | Additional CNAME records pointing at the hostname (e.g., `["www"]`) | 517 518 For AWS, `domain` must match an existing Route53 hosted zone. For DigitalOcean, `domain` must already be registered in your DigitalOcean account under Networking → Domains. 519 520 When `is_apex_domain` is `true` and `cname_aliases` includes `"www"`, creating a DNS-enabled server produces three records: 521 522 | Record | Type | Value | 523 |--------|------|-------| 524 | `web.example.com` | A | instance public IP | 525 | `example.com` | A | instance public IP | 526 | `www.example.com` | CNAME | `web.example.com` | 527 528 All records are cleaned up on delete. 529 530 ### Supported AWS operating systems 531 532 `ubuntu-24.04`, `ubuntu-22.04`, `ubuntu-20.04`, `amazon-linux-2023`, `amazon-linux-2`, `debian-12`, `debian-11` 533 534 See `examples/aws-web-server/` for a complete AWS config. 535 536 ### Supported DigitalOcean images 537 538 Any image slug available in your target region. Common Ubuntu slugs: `ubuntu-24-04-x64`, `ubuntu-22-04-x64`, `ubuntu-20-04-x64`. List available images with the DigitalOcean CLI: `doctl compute image list --public`. 539 540 See `examples/do-server/` for a complete DigitalOcean config. 541 542 ## Cloud-Init Variables 543 544 Goloo substitutes these placeholders in cloud-init YAML before passing to the provider: 545 546 | Variable | Value | 547 |----------|-------| 548 | `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` | SSH public keys of the first user (fetched from GitHub) | 549 | `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_USERNAME}` | SSH public keys for a specific user (uppercase username) | 550 551 Example with multiple users: 552 553 ```yaml 554 users: 555 - name: ubuntu 556 ssh_authorized_keys: 557 - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY} 558 - name: deploy 559 ssh_authorized_keys: 560 - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_DEPLOY} 561 ``` 562 563 ```json 564 { 565 "vm": { 566 "users": [ 567 {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "alice"}, 568 {"username": "deploy", "github_username": "bot-account"} 569 ] 570 } 571 } 572 ``` 573 574 ## DNS Swap (Blue-Green Deployment) 575 576 Deploy a new server alongside the old one, then atomically switch DNS. Works with both AWS and DigitalOcean: 577 578 ```bash 579 # AWS 580 goloo create web-server-v2 --aws 581 # test web-server-v2 ... 582 goloo dns swap web-server-v2 583 goloo delete web-server-v1 584 585 # DigitalOcean 586 goloo create web-server-v2 --do 587 # test web-server-v2 ... 588 goloo dns swap web-server-v2 589 goloo delete web-server-v1 590 ``` 591 592 `dns swap` updates all A records tracked in the VM's state to point at the current VM's public IP and re-creates any CNAME aliases. The provider is auto-detected from the state section. 593 594 ## Releasing .deb Packages with GoReleaser 595 596 Goloo uses [GoReleaser](https://goreleaser.com) to cross-compile for amd64 and arm64 and produce `.deb` packages on each tagged release. The `.deb` files are published to a GitHub Pages APT repository so that VMs provisioned by goloo can install it via `apt`. 597 598 ### How it works 599 600 1. Tag a release: `git tag v1.0.0 && git push --tags` 601 2. GitHub Actions runs GoReleaser, which cross-compiles and builds `.deb` packages for amd64 and arm64 602 3. The workflow updates the APT repository structure and deploys it to GitHub Pages 603 4. VMs can then `apt install goloo` 604 605 ### Installing goloo on a provisioned VM 606 607 Add the APT repository to your cloud-init template: 608 609 ```yaml 610 #cloud-config 611 apt: 612 sources: 613 goloo: 614 source: "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/goloo.gpg] https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo stable main" 615 keyid: YOUR_GPG_FINGERPRINT 616 keyserver: https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo/key.gpg 617 618 packages: 619 - goloo 620 ``` 621 622 This works identically on local Multipass VMs, AWS EC2 instances, and DigitalOcean Droplets — cloud-init's `apt` module handles the repository setup on all providers. 623 624 Or install manually on an existing machine: 625 626 ```bash 627 curl -fsSL https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo/key.gpg \ 628 | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/goloo.gpg > /dev/null 629 echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/goloo.gpg] https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo stable main" \ 630 | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/goloo.list 631 sudo apt update && sudo apt install goloo 632 ``` 633 634 ### Building a release locally 635 636 ```bash 637 goreleaser release --snapshot --clean 638 ``` 639 640 This produces `.deb` files in `dist/` without publishing. Useful for testing the packaging before tagging a real release. 641 642 See [docs/debian.md](docs/debian.md) for details on `.deb` file structure, GPG signing, and repository hosting options. 643 644 ## Building from Source 645 646 Requires Go 1.21+. 647 648 ```bash 649 make build # Build binary to ./bin/goloo 650 make run-tests # Run all tests 651 make clean # Remove build artifacts 652 make install # Install to ~/bin 653 ``` 654 655 ## Prerequisites 656 657 - **Local VMs**: [Multipass](https://multipass.run/) installed 658 - **AWS VMs**: AWS credentials configured (`aws configure`); a Route53 hosted zone if using DNS 659 - **DigitalOcean Droplets**: `DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN` environment variable set to a Personal Access Token with read/write scope; your domain registered under Networking → Domains if using DNS 660 661 ## References 662 663 - [docs/DESIGN.md](docs/DESIGN.md) — Architecture and diagrams 664 - [docs/PLAN.md](docs/PLAN.md) — Development phases 665 - [docs/goloo-notes.md](docs/goloo-notes.md) — Detailed design notes 666 - [docs/about-the-name.md](docs/about-the-name.md) — Why "goloo" 667 - [examples/aws-web-server/](examples/aws-web-server/) — Complete AWS config example 668 - [examples/do-server/](examples/do-server/) — Complete DigitalOcean config example 669

file.content: # Goloo Disclaimer: This works for me — that's the entire guarantee. Built with AI in the loop, so check your own biases before you love it or hate it on principle. Use at your own risk, fork freely, and don't @ me when it explodes. (But do drop me a note if it helps — pay it forward.) Provision identical servers locally or in the cloud from a single config file. ## The Problem You want to build a server. You need it running locally for development, then deploy the same thing to AWS or DigitalOcean for production. Normally this means maintaining two or three separate setups — Vagrant/Multipass configs for local, Terraform/CloudFormation for cloud — that inevitably drift apart. Goloo uses one JSON config and one cloud-init YAML to create the same server anywhere. ## About the Name **Go** + **Leeloo**. Named after the character from The Fifth Element who carries a "multipass" — the same Multipass that Canonical named their VM manager after. Goloo extends that idea: one tool, one config, anywhere you need to go. See [docs/about-the-name.md](docs/about-the-name.md) for the full story. ## Quick Start ### 1. Create a config folder Each VM config lives in its own folder under `~/.config/goloo/stacks/`. The folder contains a `config.json` and an optional `cloud-init.yaml`. Create `~/.config/goloo/stacks/web-server/config.json`: ```json { "vm": { "name": "web-server", "cpus": 2, "memory": "2G", "disk": "20G", "image": "24.04", "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} ] } } ``` ### 2. Write a cloud-init file This is a standard [cloud-init](https://cloudinit.readthedocs.io/) YAML that defines what your server looks like. Goloo fetches SSH keys from GitHub automatically using the `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` placeholder. Create `~/.config/goloo/stacks/web-server/cloud-init.yaml`: ```yaml #cloud-config users: - name: ubuntu sudo: ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL shell: /bin/bash ssh_authorized_keys: - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY} package_update: true package_upgrade: true packages: - nginx - certbot - python3-certbot-nginx - fail2ban - ufw runcmd: - ufw allow OpenSSH - ufw allow 'Nginx Full' - ufw --force enable - systemctl enable nginx - systemctl start nginx ``` ### 3. Create the server locally ```bash goloo create web-server ``` This launches a Multipass VM with nginx, certbot, and fail2ban installed. SSH in with: ```bash goloo ssh web-server ``` ### 4. Deploy the same server to AWS Add AWS-specific fields to `config.json`: ```json { "vm": { "name": "web-server", "instance_type": "t3.small", "os": "ubuntu-24.04", "region": "us-east-1", "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} ] }, "dns": { "hostname": "web", "domain": "example.com", "ttl": 300 } } ``` Then create the instance: ```bash goloo create web-server --aws ``` Goloo creates a CloudFormation stack with an EC2 instance, security group, and (if a `dns` section is present) a Route53 A record pointing `web.example.com` at the instance IP. See `examples/aws-web-server/` for a complete config with all AWS parameters. ### 5. Deploy the same server to DigitalOcean Add DigitalOcean-specific fields to `config.json`: ```json { "vm": { "name": "web-server", "do_size": "s-2vcpu-4gb", "do_region": "nyc3", "do_image": "ubuntu-24-04-x64", "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} ] }, "dns": { "hostname": "web", "domain": "example.com", "ttl": 300 } } ``` Export your DigitalOcean API token and create the droplet: ```bash export DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN=your_token_here goloo create web-server --do ``` Goloo creates a Droplet, attaches a firewall, and (if a `dns` section is present) creates DNS A records in your DigitalOcean domain. See `examples/do-server/` for a complete DigitalOcean config. ## Example: Building a Development Server A more complete example — a polyglot dev server with Docker, Go, Node.js, and Python: `~/.config/goloo/stacks/dev/config.json`: ```json { "vm": { "name": "dev", "cpus": 4, "memory": "4G", "disk": "40G", "image": "24.04", "instance_type": "t3.medium", "do_size": "s-4vcpu-8gb", "do_region": "nyc3", "do_image": "ubuntu-24-04-x64", "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} ] } } ``` Several cloud-init templates are included in `configs/` for common setups. Copy one to `~/.config/goloo/stacks/dev/cloud-init.yaml`: | Config | What it installs | |--------|-----------------| | `configs/base.yaml` | curl, wget, vim, htop, git | | `configs/dev.yaml` | build-essential, ripgrep, tmux, fzf, jq | | `configs/python-dev.yaml` | Python 3, pip, venv, uv | | `configs/go-dev.yaml` | Go 1.23, gopls, delve debugger | | `configs/node-dev.yaml` | Node.js 22, npm, pnpm, yarn | | `configs/claude-dev.yaml` | All of the above plus Docker | Develop locally: ```bash goloo create dev goloo ssh dev ``` When it's ready for the cloud: ```bash goloo create dev --aws # AWS EC2 goloo create dev --do # DigitalOcean Droplet ``` ## Commands ``` goloo create Create a local VM (Multipass) goloo create --aws Create an AWS EC2 instance goloo create --do Create a DigitalOcean Droplet goloo delete Delete VM (auto-detects provider) goloo list List local VMs goloo list --aws List AWS VMs goloo list --do List DigitalOcean Droplets goloo ssh SSH into VM goloo status Show VM status goloo stop Stop VM goloo start Start VM goloo dns swap Update DNS records to current VM IP (AWS and DigitalOcean) ``` ### Flags | Flag | Description | |------|-------------| | `--aws` | Use AWS provider | | `--do`, `--digitalocean` | Use DigitalOcean provider | | `--local` | Use local Multipass provider | | `--dir PATH` | Goloo config directory (default: `~/.config/goloo`); stacks at `/stacks/` | | `--folder`, `-f PATH` | Full path to stacks folder (overrides `--dir`) | | `--users`, `-u USERS` | GitHub usernames for SSH key injection (comma-separated) | | `--verbose`, `-v` | Show detailed progress | | `--version` | Show version | | `--help`, `-h` | Show help | Flags can go in any order: ```bash goloo create web-server --aws goloo create --do web-server -f ~/my-servers goloo create web-server -u gherlein goloo create web-server -u "alice,bob" ``` ### The `--users` flag The `--users`/`-u` flag provides GitHub usernames whose SSH public keys are fetched and injected into the cloud-init template. This overrides any users defined in the config JSON. ```bash goloo create web-server -f ./examples/ -u gherlein ``` The first username maps to the `ubuntu` VM user (the default for Multipass and most cloud-init templates). Additional usernames become both the VM user and the GitHub lookup: ```bash goloo create web-server -u "alice,deploy-bot" # alice → VM user "ubuntu", SSH keys from github.com/alice.keys # deploy-bot → VM user "deploy-bot", SSH keys from github.com/deploy-bot.keys ``` ### Environment Variables | Variable | Description | |----------|-------------| | `GOLOO_STACK_FOLDER` | Default base folder for configs (overridden by `--folder`/`-f`) | | `DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN` | DigitalOcean API token (required when using `--do`) | Precedence for stack folder: `--folder`/`-f` > `--dir` > `GOLOO_STACK_FOLDER` > `~/.config/goloo/stacks/` `DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN` is read at runtime and never written to disk or logged. ### Provider Auto-Detection When you don't pass `--aws`, `--do`, or `--local`, goloo detects the provider from saved state: 1. Config has a `do` state section (previously created with `--do`) → DigitalOcean 2. Config has an `aws` state section (previously created with `--aws`) → AWS 3. Otherwise → Multipass A config can include a `dns` section without triggering a cloud provider — DNS records are only created when you explicitly pass `--aws` or `--do`. This means `goloo delete web-server` does the right thing regardless of where the VM was created. ### Legacy Flags For users migrating from [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2): ```bash goloo -c -n web-server # Same as: goloo create web-server --aws goloo -d -n web-server # Same as: goloo delete web-server --aws ``` ## Config File Each VM's configuration lives in a named folder under a base directory (default `~/.config/goloo/stacks/`): ``` ~/.config/goloo/stacks/ └── web-server/ ├── config.json └── cloud-init.yaml ``` The `config.json` file has two types of sections: **input sections** that you write by hand, and **state sections** that goloo manages automatically. ### Input vs State | Section | Type | Who writes it | When | |---------|------|---------------|------| | `vm` | Input | You | Before first `goloo create` | | `dns` | Input | You | Before first `goloo create` | | `local` | State | Goloo | Written by `goloo create`, removed by `goloo delete` | | `aws` | State | Goloo | Written by `goloo create --aws`, removed by `goloo delete` | | `do` | State | Goloo | Written by `goloo create --do`, removed by `goloo delete` | Input sections (`vm`, `dns`) are never modified by goloo. They define what you want. State sections (`local`, `aws`, `do`) track what goloo created, so it knows how to manage and clean up resources later. Because input and state are separate, the same config supports simultaneous local and cloud deployments. Creating a local VM writes a `local` section; creating an AWS instance writes an `aws` section; creating a DigitalOcean Droplet writes a `do` section. Deleting one removes only its state section without touching the others. ### Minimal config A config file needs only a `vm` section with a name and at least one user: ```json { "vm": { "name": "devbox", "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "your-github-username"} ] } } ``` Everything else has defaults. This creates a local VM with 2 CPUs, 2G RAM, 20G disk, Ubuntu 24.04. ### Full config example This shows all input fields across all providers: ```json { "vm": { "name": "web-server", "cpus": 4, "memory": "4G", "disk": "40G", "image": "24.04", "instance_type": "t3.small", "os": "ubuntu-24.04", "region": "us-east-1", "vpc_id": "", "subnet_id": "", "do_size": "s-2vcpu-4gb", "do_region": "nyc3", "do_image": "ubuntu-24-04-x64", "do_vpc_id": "", "ssh_cidr": "0.0.0.0/0", "web_cidr": "0.0.0.0/0", "mounts": [ {"source": "/home/user/code", "target": "/home/ubuntu/code"} ], "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "alice"}, {"username": "deploy", "github_username": "deploy-bot"} ] }, "dns": { "hostname": "web", "domain": "example.com", "ttl": 300, "zone_id": "", "is_apex_domain": true, "cname_aliases": ["www"] } } ``` Fields are provider-specific — each provider ignores fields it doesn't use (see the reference tables below). ### After creating a local VM After `goloo create web-server`, goloo adds a `local` section: ```json { "vm": { ... }, "local": { "ip": "192.168.64.5" } } ``` After `goloo delete web-server`, the `local` section is removed entirely. ### After creating an AWS instance After `goloo create web-server --aws`, goloo adds an `aws` section with all the resources it provisioned: ```json { "vm": { ... }, "dns": { ... }, "aws": { "public_ip": "54.1.2.3", "instance_id": "i-0123456789abcdef0", "stack_id": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456:stack/goloo-web-server/abc", "stack_name": "goloo-web-server", "security_group": "sg-abc123", "ami_id": "ami-0123456789abcdef0", "vpc_id": "vpc-abc123", "subnet_id": "subnet-def456", "zone_id": "Z1234567890", "fqdn": "web.example.com", "dns_records": [ {"name": "web.example.com", "type": "A", "value": "54.1.2.3", "ttl": 300}, {"name": "example.com", "type": "A", "value": "54.1.2.3", "ttl": 300}, {"name": "www.example.com", "type": "CNAME", "value": "web.example.com", "ttl": 300} ] } } ``` After `goloo delete web-server`, the entire `aws` section is removed. The `vm` and `dns` input sections stay intact so you can re-create the server without editing the config. If goloo created a VPC and subnet (because no default VPC existed), the `aws` section also tracks those resources so they are cleaned up on delete: ```json { "aws": { "created_vpc": true, "created_subnet": true, "internet_gateway_id": "igw-789", "route_table_id": "rtb-abc", "route_table_association_id": "rtbassoc-def", ... } } ``` ### After creating a DigitalOcean Droplet After `goloo create web-server --do`, goloo adds a `do` section: ```json { "vm": { ... }, "dns": { ... }, "do": { "droplet_id": 123456789, "public_ip": "192.241.0.1", "fqdn": "web.example.com", "firewall_id": "bb4b2611-3d72-467b-8602-280330ecd65c", "dns_records": [ {"name": "web", "type": "A", "value": "192.241.0.1", "ttl": 300, "record_id": 12345}, {"name": "@", "type": "A", "value": "192.241.0.1", "ttl": 300, "record_id": 12346}, {"name": "www", "type": "CNAME", "value": "web.example.com.", "ttl": 300, "record_id": 12347} ] } } ``` After `goloo delete web-server`, goloo deletes the Droplet, firewall, and DNS records, then removes the `do` section. The `vm` and `dns` input sections are preserved. ### Simultaneous deployments Because state is stored in separate sections, multiple providers can be active at once: ```json { "vm": { ... }, "dns": { ... }, "local": { "ip": "192.168.64.5" }, "aws": { "public_ip": "54.1.2.3", ... }, "do": { "public_ip": "192.241.0.1", ... } } ``` Delete each independently: ```bash goloo delete web-server --local # removes local section only goloo delete web-server --aws # removes aws section only goloo delete web-server --do # removes do section only ``` ### vm section reference | Field | Default | Provider | Description | |-------|---------|----------|-------------| | `name` | (required) | All | VM name, used for stack naming and config lookup | | `cpus` | 2 | Local | Number of CPUs | | `memory` | `"2G"` | Local | RAM allocation | | `disk` | `"20G"` | Local | Disk size | | `image` | `"24.04"` | Local | Ubuntu version for Multipass | | `mounts` | | Local | List of `{"source", "target"}` host directory mounts | | `instance_type` | `"t3.micro"` | AWS | EC2 instance type | | `os` | `"ubuntu-24.04"` | AWS | AMI lookup key | | `region` | `"us-east-1"` | AWS | AWS region | | `vpc_id` | | AWS | Specific VPC to use (auto-discovered if empty) | | `subnet_id` | | AWS | Specific subnet to use (auto-discovered if empty) | | `do_size` | | DigitalOcean | Droplet size slug (e.g., `"s-2vcpu-4gb"`) | | `do_region` | | DigitalOcean | Region slug (e.g., `"nyc3"`, `"sfo3"`, `"lon1"`) | | `do_image` | | DigitalOcean | Image slug (e.g., `"ubuntu-24-04-x64"`) | | `do_vpc_id` | | DigitalOcean | VPC UUID to launch the Droplet into (optional) | | `ssh_cidr` | `"0.0.0.0/0"` | DigitalOcean | CIDR allowed to reach port 22 on the Droplet firewall | | `web_cidr` | `"0.0.0.0/0"` | DigitalOcean | CIDR allowed to reach ports 80 and 443 on the Droplet firewall | | `users` | (required) | All | List of `{"username", "github_username"}` for SSH key injection | ### dns section reference (AWS and DigitalOcean) | Field | Default | Description | |-------|---------|-------------| | `hostname` | `vm.name` | Hostname portion of the FQDN | | `domain` | | Hosted zone domain (e.g., `"example.com"`) | | `ttl` | 300 | DNS record TTL in seconds | | `zone_id` | | Route53 hosted zone ID (AWS only; auto-looked up from domain if empty) | | `is_apex_domain` | `false` | Also create an A record at the zone apex (bare domain) | | `cname_aliases` | | Additional CNAME records pointing at the hostname (e.g., `["www"]`) | For AWS, `domain` must match an existing Route53 hosted zone. For DigitalOcean, `domain` must already be registered in your DigitalOcean account under Networking → Domains. When `is_apex_domain` is `true` and `cname_aliases` includes `"www"`, creating a DNS-enabled server produces three records: | Record | Type | Value | |--------|------|-------| | `web.example.com` | A | instance public IP | | `example.com` | A | instance public IP | | `www.example.com` | CNAME | `web.example.com` | All records are cleaned up on delete. ### Supported AWS operating systems `ubuntu-24.04`, `ubuntu-22.04`, `ubuntu-20.04`, `amazon-linux-2023`, `amazon-linux-2`, `debian-12`, `debian-11` See `examples/aws-web-server/` for a complete AWS config. ### Supported DigitalOcean images Any image slug available in your target region. Common Ubuntu slugs: `ubuntu-24-04-x64`, `ubuntu-22-04-x64`, `ubuntu-20-04-x64`. List available images with the DigitalOcean CLI: `doctl compute image list --public`. See `examples/do-server/` for a complete DigitalOcean config. ## Cloud-Init Variables Goloo substitutes these placeholders in cloud-init YAML before passing to the provider: | Variable | Value | |----------|-------| | `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` | SSH public keys of the first user (fetched from GitHub) | | `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_USERNAME}` | SSH public keys for a specific user (uppercase username) | Example with multiple users: ```yaml users: - name: ubuntu ssh_authorized_keys: - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY} - name: deploy ssh_authorized_keys: - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_DEPLOY} ``` ```json { "vm": { "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "alice"}, {"username": "deploy", "github_username": "bot-account"} ] } } ``` ## DNS Swap (Blue-Green Deployment) Deploy a new server alongside the old one, then atomically switch DNS. Works with both AWS and DigitalOcean: ```bash # AWS goloo create web-server-v2 --aws # test web-server-v2 ... goloo dns swap web-server-v2 goloo delete web-server-v1 # DigitalOcean goloo create web-server-v2 --do # test web-server-v2 ... goloo dns swap web-server-v2 goloo delete web-server-v1 ``` `dns swap` updates all A records tracked in the VM's state to point at the current VM's public IP and re-creates any CNAME aliases. The provider is auto-detected from the state section. ## Releasing .deb Packages with GoReleaser Goloo uses [GoReleaser](https://goreleaser.com) to cross-compile for amd64 and arm64 and produce `.deb` packages on each tagged release. The `.deb` files are published to a GitHub Pages APT repository so that VMs provisioned by goloo can install it via `apt`. ### How it works 1. Tag a release: `git tag v1.0.0 && git push --tags` 2. GitHub Actions runs GoReleaser, which cross-compiles and builds `.deb` packages for amd64 and arm64 3. The workflow updates the APT repository structure and deploys it to GitHub Pages 4. VMs can then `apt install goloo` ### Installing goloo on a provisioned VM Add the APT repository to your cloud-init template: ```yaml #cloud-config apt: sources: goloo: source: "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/goloo.gpg] https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo stable main" keyid: YOUR_GPG_FINGERPRINT keyserver: https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo/key.gpg packages: - goloo ``` This works identically on local Multipass VMs, AWS EC2 instances, and DigitalOcean Droplets — cloud-init's `apt` module handles the repository setup on all providers. Or install manually on an existing machine: ```bash curl -fsSL https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo/key.gpg \ | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/goloo.gpg > /dev/null echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/goloo.gpg] https://emergingrobotics.github.io/goloo stable main" \ | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/goloo.list sudo apt update && sudo apt install goloo ``` ### Building a release locally ```bash goreleaser release --snapshot --clean ``` This produces `.deb` files in `dist/` without publishing. Useful for testing the packaging before tagging a real release. See [docs/debian.md](docs/debian.md) for details on `.deb` file structure, GPG signing, and repository hosting options. ## Building from Source Requires Go 1.21+. ```bash make build # Build binary to ./bin/goloo make run-tests # Run all tests make clean # Remove build artifacts make install # Install to ~/bin ``` ## Prerequisites - **Local VMs**: [Multipass](https://multipass.run/) installed - **AWS VMs**: AWS credentials configured (`aws configure`); a Route53 hosted zone if using DNS - **DigitalOcean Droplets**: `DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN` environment variable set to a Personal Access Token with read/write scope; your domain registered under Networking → Domains if using DNS ## References - [docs/DESIGN.md](docs/DESIGN.md) — Architecture and diagrams - [docs/PLAN.md](docs/PLAN.md) — Development phases - [docs/goloo-notes.md](docs/goloo-notes.md) — Detailed design notes - [docs/about-the-name.md](docs/about-the-name.md) — Why "goloo" - [examples/aws-web-server/](examples/aws-web-server/) — Complete AWS config example - [examples/do-server/](examples/do-server/) — Complete DigitalOcean config example

content.content:
1	# Goloo Design Document
2	
3	Unified CLI tool for provisioning VMs locally (Multipass) or in the cloud (AWS) using the same configuration and cloud-init files.
4	
5	## Problem Statement
6	
7	Development teams need consistent environments across local machines and cloud infrastructure. Currently:
8	
9	1. **Local VMs** use Multipass with cloud-init YAML
10	2. **Cloud VMs** use AWS CLI/Console with the same cloud-init YAML
11	3. **No unified CLI** bridges these environments with identical configs
12	
13	The cloud-init format is already portable. The missing piece is a single tool that abstracts provider differences while keeping configs identical.
14	
15	## Solution
16	
17	Goloo provides a unified interface:
18	
19	```bash
20	goloo create devbox              # Local VM (Multipass)
21	goloo create devbox --aws        # AWS EC2 instance
22	```
23	
24	Same config file, same cloud-init, different targets.
25	
26	## Core Principle
27	
28	**One config, any target.** A goloo config file plus a cloud-init YAML should:
29	1. Create a local VM via `goloo create myvm`
30	2. Create an identical AWS EC2 instance via `goloo create myvm --aws`
31	
32	The developer workflow is: develop locally, deploy to cloud with a flag change.
33	
34	---
35	
36	## System Architecture
37	
38	### Block Diagram
39	
40	```mermaid
41	flowchart TB
42	    subgraph CLI["CLI Layer"]
43	        CMD[Command Parser]
44	        CFG[Config Loader]
45	    end
46	
47	    subgraph Core["Core Layer"]
48	        DISP[Provider Dispatcher]
49	        CINIT[Cloud-Init Processor]
50	        SSH[SSH Key Fetcher]
51	    end
52	
53	    subgraph Providers["Provider Layer"]
54	        MP[Multipass Provider]
55	        AWS[AWS Provider]
56	    end
57	
58	    subgraph MultipassExt["Multipass External"]
59	        MPBIN[multipass CLI]
60	    end
61	
62	    subgraph AWSExt["AWS External"]
63	        CFN[CloudFormation]
64	        R53[Route53]
65	        SSM[SSM Parameter Store]
66	        EC2[EC2]
67	    end
68	
69	    subgraph Storage["Storage"]
70	        STACKS[(~/.config/goloo/stacks/name/config.json)]
71	        CLOUDINIT[(~/.config/goloo/stacks/name/cloud-init.yaml)]
72	    end
73	
74	    subgraph External["External APIs"]
75	        GH[GitHub API]
76	    end
77	
78	    CMD --> CFG
79	    CFG --> STACKS
80	    CFG --> DISP
81	    DISP --> CINIT
82	    CINIT --> CLOUDINIT
83	    CINIT --> SSH
84	    SSH --> GH
85	
86	    DISP --> MP
87	    DISP --> AWS
88	
89	    MP --> MPBIN
90	
91	    AWS --> CFN
92	    AWS --> R53
93	    AWS --> SSM
94	    AWS --> EC2
95	```
96	
97	### Component Responsibilities
98	
99	| Component | Responsibility |
100	|-----------|----------------|
101	| Command Parser | Parse CLI flags (`create`, `delete`, `--aws`, etc.) |
102	| Config Loader | Read JSON configs from `~/.config/goloo/stacks/<name>/config.json`, apply defaults, validate |
103	| Provider Dispatcher | Route to Multipass or AWS based on flags/config |
104	| Cloud-Init Processor | Substitute `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` variables, validate YAML |
105	| SSH Key Fetcher | Fetch public keys from `https://github.com/{user}.keys` |
106	| Multipass Provider | Shell out to `multipass` CLI, parse JSON output |
107	| AWS Provider | Use AWS SDK for CloudFormation, Route53, SSM, EC2 |
108

110	
111	## How It Works
112	
113	### Local VMs (Multipass)
114	
115	Goloo shells out to `multipass`. The config file translates directly to CLI parameters:
116	
117	```
118	goloo config          →    multipass command
119	─────────────────────────────────────────────
120	vm.name               →    --name
121	vm.cpus               →    --cpus
122	vm.memory             →    --memory
123	vm.disk               →    --disk
124	vm.image              →    (positional arg)
125	cloud-init.yaml       →    --cloud-init
126	```
127	
128	The cloud-init YAML passes through unchanged after SSH key substitution.
129	
130	### AWS VMs
131	
132	Goloo uses the AWS Go SDK (based on [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2)) to:
133	
134	1. Look up AMI ID via SSM Parameter Store
135	2. Discover or create VPC/Subnet infrastructure
136	3. Create CloudFormation stack (EC2 instance + security group)
137	4. Pass cloud-init YAML as EC2 UserData (base64 encoded)
138	5. Create Route53 DNS record: `{hostname}.{domain}` → instance IP
139	6. Update config file with output fields (instance_id, public_ip, etc.)
140

143	## Data Flow
144	
145	### VM Creation Sequence (Local)
146	
147	```mermaid
148	sequenceDiagram
149	    participant User
150	    participant CLI
151	    participant Config
152	    participant CloudInit
153	    participant GitHub
154	    participant Multipass
155	
156	    User->>CLI: goloo create devbox
157	    CLI->>Config: Load ~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/config.json
158	    Config-->>CLI: VMConfig
159	
160	    CLI->>CloudInit: Process(~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/cloud-init.yaml, users)
161	    CloudInit->>GitHub: GET /gherlein.keys
162	    GitHub-->>CloudInit: SSH public keys
163	    CloudInit->>CloudInit: Substitute ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}
164	    CloudInit-->>CLI: Processed YAML path
165	
166	    CLI->>Multipass: multipass launch 24.04 --name devbox --cpus 4 --memory 4G --disk 40G --cloud-init /tmp/processed.yaml
167	    Multipass-->>CLI: VM created
168	
169	    CLI->>Multipass: multipass info devbox --format json
170	    Multipass-->>CLI: {"info":{"devbox":{"ipv4":["192.168.64.5"]}}}
171	
172	    CLI->>Config: Update config with IP
173	    CLI-->>User: VM created at 192.168.64.5
174	```
175	
176	### VM Creation Sequence (AWS)
177	
178	```mermaid
179	sequenceDiagram
180	    participant User
181	    participant CLI
182	    participant Config
183	    participant CloudInit
184	    participant GitHub
185	    participant SSM
186	    participant EC2
187	    participant CFN
188	    participant R53
189	
190	    User->>CLI: goloo create devbox --aws
191	    CLI->>Config: Load ~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/config.json
192	    Config-->>CLI: VMConfig + DNSConfig
193	
194	    CLI->>CloudInit: Process(~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/cloud-init.yaml, users)
195	    CloudInit->>GitHub: GET /gherlein.keys
196	    GitHub-->>CloudInit: SSH public keys
197	    CloudInit-->>CLI: Processed cloud-init content
198	
199	    CLI->>SSM: GetParameter(/aws/service/canonical/ubuntu/...)
200	    SSM-->>CLI: ami-0123456789
201	
202	    CLI->>EC2: DescribeVpcs (find default VPC)
203	    EC2-->>CLI: vpc-abc123
204	    CLI->>EC2: DescribeSubnets
205	    EC2-->>CLI: subnet-def456
206	
207	    CLI->>CFN: CreateStack(template, params, UserData)
208	    CFN-->>CLI: StackId
209	
210	    CLI->>CFN: Wait for CREATE_COMPLETE
211	    CFN-->>CLI: Success
212	
213	    CLI->>CFN: DescribeStacks (get outputs)
214	    CFN-->>CLI: InstanceId, PublicIP, SecurityGroupId
215	
216	    CLI->>R53: ListHostedZonesByName(domain)
217	    R53-->>CLI: ZoneId
218	    CLI->>R53: ChangeResourceRecordSets (A record)
219	    R53-->>CLI: Success
220	
221	    CLI->>Config: Update config with outputs
222	    CLI-->>User: VM created at devbox.example.com (54.1.2.3)
223	```
224	
225	### VM Deletion Sequence
226	
227	```mermaid
228	sequenceDiagram
229	    participant User
230	    participant CLI
231	    participant Config
232	    participant Provider
233	    participant External
234	
235	    User->>CLI: goloo delete devbox
236	    CLI->>Config: Load ~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/config.json
237	    Config-->>CLI: Config (with provider detection)
238	
239	    alt Multipass (no stack_id)
240	        CLI->>Provider: Delete via multipass
241	        Provider->>External: multipass delete devbox
242	        External-->>Provider: OK
243	        Provider->>External: multipass purge
244	        External-->>Provider: OK
245	    else AWS (has stack_id)
246	        CLI->>Provider: Delete via AWS
247	        Provider->>External: Route53 DELETE records
248	        External-->>Provider: OK
249	        Provider->>External: CloudFormation DeleteStack
250	        External-->>Provider: Deletion initiated
251	        Provider->>External: Wait for DELETE_COMPLETE
252	        External-->>Provider: OK
253	        Provider->>External: Delete created VPC/Subnet (if any)
254	        External-->>Provider: OK
255	    end
256	
257	    CLI->>Config: Clear output fields
258	    CLI-->>User: VM deleted
259	```

478	## Cloud-Init Processing
479	
480	### Variable Substitution Flow
481	
482	```mermaid
483	flowchart LR
484	    A[~/.config/goloo/stacks/name/cloud-init.yaml] --> B[Read Template]
485	    B --> C{Has Users?}
486	    C -->|Yes| D[Fetch GitHub Keys]
487	    D --> E[github.com/user.keys]
488	    E --> F[Substitute Variables]
489	    C -->|No| F
490	    F --> G[Write Temp File]
491	    G --> H[Return Path]
492	
493	    subgraph Variables
494	        V1["${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}"]
495	        V2["${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_USERNAME}"]
496	    end
497	```
498	
499	### Supported Variables
500	
501	| Variable | Source | Description |
502	|----------|--------|-------------|
503	| `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` | GitHub | First user's SSH public keys |
504	| `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_<USERNAME>}` | GitHub | Specific user's SSH keys (uppercase) |
505	
506	### Example Cloud-Init
507	
508	```yaml
509	#cloud-config
510	users:
511	  - name: ubuntu
512	    groups: sudo
513	    shell: /bin/bash
514	    sudo: ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
515	    ssh_authorized_keys:
516	      - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}
517	
518	packages:
519	  - git
520	  - build-essential
521	  - golang-go
522	
523	runcmd:
524	  - echo "Cloud-init complete" > /var/log/goloo-init.log
525	```

529	## AWS Implementation Details
530	
531	### CloudFormation Template
532	
533	Based on [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2), the generated template includes:
534	
535	```yaml
536	AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
537	Description: EC2 instance with SSH access
538	
539	Parameters:
540	  ImageId:
541	    Type: String
542	  InstanceType:
543	    Type: String
544	    Default: t3.micro
545	  VpcId:
546	    Type: String
547	  SubnetId:
548	    Type: String
549	
550	Resources:
551	  SSHSecurityGroup:
552	    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
553	    Properties:
554	      GroupDescription: Allow SSH/HTTP/HTTPS
555	      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
556	      SecurityGroupIngress:
557	        - IpProtocol: tcp
558	          FromPort: 22
559	          ToPort: 22
560	          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
561	        - IpProtocol: tcp
562	          FromPort: 80
563	          ToPort: 80
564	          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
565	        - IpProtocol: tcp
566	          FromPort: 443
567	          ToPort: 443
568	          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
569	
570	  EC2Instance:
571	    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
572	    Properties:
573	      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
574	      ImageId: !Ref ImageId
575	      NetworkInterfaces:
576	        - DeviceIndex: "0"
577	          SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
578	          AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
579	          GroupSet:
580	            - !GetAtt SSHSecurityGroup.GroupId
581	      UserData: <base64-encoded-cloud-init>
582	
583	Outputs:
584	  InstanceId:
585	    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
586	  PublicIP:
587	    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicIp
588	  SecurityGroupId:
589	    Value: !Ref SSHSecurityGroup
590	```
591	
592	### AMI Lookup via SSM
593	
594	```go
595	var osSSMPaths = map[string]string{
596	    "ubuntu-24.04": "/aws/service/canonical/ubuntu/server/24.04/stable/current/amd64/hvm/ebs-gp2/ami-id",
597	    "ubuntu-22.04": "/aws/service/canonical/ubuntu/server/22.04/stable/current/amd64/hvm/ebs-gp2/ami-id",
598	    "amazon-linux-2023": "/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-x86_64",
599	}
600	```
601	
602	### VPC/Subnet Discovery
603	
604	1. Try to find default VPC
605	2. If no default VPC, find any available VPC
606	3. If no VPC exists, create network stack (VPC + Subnet + IGW + Route Table)
607	4. Find subnet with public IP auto-assign, or create one
608	
609	### DNS Record Creation
610	
611	1. Look up hosted zone ID for domain
612	2. Create A record: `hostname.domain` → public IP
613	3. Optionally create CNAME aliases
614	4. Optionally create apex domain record

721	## Update Strategy: CNAME Swap
722	
723	For zero-downtime updates, goloo supports blue-green deployment via DNS:
724	
725	```mermaid
726	sequenceDiagram
727	    participant User
728	    participant DNS
729	    participant Blue as VM (Blue v1.0)
730	    participant Green as VM (Green v1.1)
731	
732	    Note over Blue: Running v1.0
733	    User->>DNS: app.example.com
734	    DNS->>Blue: 10.0.1.100
735	
736	    User->>Green: goloo create app-v2 --aws
737	    Note over Green: Deploy v1.1
738	    Green->>Green: Cloud-init runs
739	
740	    User->>Green: Test green VM
741	
742	    User->>DNS: goloo dns swap app
743	    Note over DNS: Update A record
744	    DNS->>DNS: app.example.com → 10.0.2.200
745	
746	    User->>DNS: app.example.com
747	    DNS->>Green: 10.0.2.200
748	
749	    Note over Blue: Keep 1hr for rollback
750	    User->>Blue: goloo delete app-v1
751	```
