809	
810	    P --> P1[Multipass not installed]
811	    P --> P2[AWS credentials missing]
812	    P --> P3[Resource already exists]
813	    P --> P4[Resource not found]
814	    P --> P5[VPC/Subnet not found]
815	
816	    N --> N1[GitHub unreachable]
817	    N --> N2[AWS API timeout]
818	    N --> N3[CloudFormation failed]
819	
820	    C --> C1[Config file not found]
821	    C --> C2[Invalid JSON]
822	    C --> C3[Unsupported OS]
823	```
824	
825	### Error Messages
826	
827	All errors should be actionable:
828	
829	```go
830	var (
831	    ErrConfigNotFound   = errors.New("config file not found: create ~/.config/goloo/stacks/<name>/config.json")
832	    ErrProviderUnknown  = errors.New("unknown provider: use 'multipass' or 'aws'")
833	    ErrVMExists         = errors.New("VM already exists: delete first with 'goloo delete <name>'")
834	    ErrVMNotFound       = errors.New("VM not found: check 'goloo list' for available VMs")
835	    ErrNoSSHKeys        = errors.New("no SSH keys found: verify GitHub username at github.com/<user>.keys")
836	    ErrNoVPC            = errors.New("no VPC found: will create new network infrastructure")
837	    ErrAWSCredentials   = errors.New("AWS credentials not configured: run 'aws configure'")
838	    ErrMultipassMissing = errors.New("multipass not installed: visit multipass.run")
839	)
840	```
841	
842	---
843	
844	## Security Considerations
845	
846	1. **SSH Keys**: Fetched from GitHub over HTTPS only
847	2. **AWS Credentials**: Use standard AWS credential chain (env, ~/.aws, IAM role)
848	3. **Config Files**: May contain sensitive data - gitignored by default
849	4. **Cloud-Init**: Runs as root - review templates carefully
850	5. **Security Groups**: SSH open to 0.0.0.0/0 by default - restrict for production
851	6. **DNS TTL**: Set low (60-300s) to enable quick CNAME swaps
852	
853	---
854	
855	## Dependencies
856	
857	### Go Modules
858	
859	```go
860	require (
861	    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2 v1.x
862	    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config v1.x
863	    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/cloudformation v1.x
864	    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/ec2 v1.x
865	    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/route53 v1.x
866	    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/ssm v1.x
867	)
868	```
869	
870	### External Dependencies
871	
872	| Dependency | Required For |
873	|------------|--------------|
874	| `multipass` CLI | Local VMs |
875	| AWS credentials | AWS provider |
876	| Internet access | GitHub SSH keys, AWS API |
877	
878	---
879	
880	## References
881	
882	- [Multipass Documentation](https://multipass.run/)
883	- [cloud-init Documentation](https://cloudinit.readthedocs.io/)
884	- [AWS CloudFormation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cloudformation/)
885	- [aws-ec2 Repository](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2) - Foundation for AWS provider
886	- [AWS SDK for Go v2](https://aws.github.io/aws-sdk-go-v2/)
887

file.content:
# Goloo Design Document

Unified CLI tool for provisioning VMs locally (Multipass) or in the cloud (AWS) using the same configuration and cloud-init files.

## Problem Statement

Development teams need consistent environments across local machines and cloud infrastructure. Currently:

1. **Local VMs** use Multipass with cloud-init YAML
2. **Cloud VMs** use AWS CLI/Console with the same cloud-init YAML
3. **No unified CLI** bridges these environments with identical configs

The cloud-init format is already portable. The missing piece is a single tool that abstracts provider differences while keeping configs identical.

## Solution

Goloo provides a unified interface:

```bash
goloo create devbox              # Local VM (Multipass)
goloo create devbox --aws        # AWS EC2 instance
```

Same config file, same cloud-init, different targets.

## Core Principle

**One config, any target.** A goloo config file plus a cloud-init YAML should:
1. Create a local VM via `goloo create myvm`
2. Create an identical AWS EC2 instance via `goloo create myvm --aws`

The developer workflow is: develop locally, deploy to cloud with a flag change.

---

## System Architecture

### Block Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph CLI["CLI Layer"]
        CMD[Command Parser]
        CFG[Config Loader]
    end

    subgraph Core["Core Layer"]
        DISP[Provider Dispatcher]
        CINIT[Cloud-Init Processor]
        SSH[SSH Key Fetcher]
    end

    subgraph Providers["Provider Layer"]
        MP[Multipass Provider]
        AWS[AWS Provider]
    end

    subgraph MultipassExt["Multipass External"]
        MPBIN[multipass CLI]
    end

    subgraph AWSExt["AWS External"]
        CFN[CloudFormation]
        R53[Route53]
        SSM[SSM Parameter Store]
        EC2[EC2]
    end

    subgraph Storage["Storage"]
        STACKS[(~/.config/goloo/stacks/name/config.json)]
        CLOUDINIT[(~/.config/goloo/stacks/name/cloud-init.yaml)]
    end

    subgraph External["External APIs"]
        GH[GitHub API]
    end

    CMD --> CFG
    CFG --> STACKS
    CFG --> DISP
    DISP --> CINIT
    CINIT --> CLOUDINIT
    CINIT --> SSH
    SSH --> GH

    DISP --> MP
    DISP --> AWS

    MP --> MPBIN

    AWS --> CFN
    AWS --> R53
    AWS --> SSM
    AWS --> EC2
```

### Component Responsibilities

| Component | Responsibility |
|-----------|----------------|
| Command Parser | Parse CLI flags (`create`, `delete`, `--aws`, etc.) |
| Config Loader | Read JSON configs from `~/.config/goloo/stacks/<name>/config.json`, apply defaults, validate |
| Provider Dispatcher | Route to Multipass or AWS based on flags/config |
| Cloud-Init Processor | Substitute `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` variables, validate YAML |
| SSH Key Fetcher | Fetch public keys from `https://github.com/{user}.keys` |
| Multipass Provider | Shell out to `multipass` CLI, parse JSON output |
| AWS Provider | Use AWS SDK for CloudFormation, Route53, SSM, EC2 |

---

## How It Works

### Local VMs (Multipass)

Goloo shells out to `multipass`. The config file translates directly to CLI parameters:

```
goloo config          →    multipass command
─────────────────────────────────────────────
vm.name               →    --name
vm.cpus               →    --cpus
vm.memory             →    --memory
vm.disk               →    --disk
vm.image              →    (positional arg)
cloud-init.yaml       →    --cloud-init
```

The cloud-init YAML passes through unchanged after SSH key substitution.

### AWS VMs

Goloo uses the AWS Go SDK (based on [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2)) to:

1. Look up AMI ID via SSM Parameter Store
2. Discover or create VPC/Subnet infrastructure
3. Create CloudFormation stack (EC2 instance + security group)
4. Pass cloud-init YAML as EC2 UserData (base64 encoded)
5. Create Route53 DNS record: `{hostname}.{domain}` → instance IP
6. Update config file with output fields (instance_id, public_ip, etc.)

---

## Data Flow

### VM Creation Sequence (Local)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant CLI
    participant Config
    participant CloudInit
    participant GitHub
    participant Multipass

    User->>CLI: goloo create devbox
    CLI->>Config: Load ~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/config.json
    Config-->>CLI: VMConfig

    CLI->>CloudInit: Process(~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/cloud-init.yaml, users)
    CloudInit->>GitHub: GET /gherlein.keys
    GitHub-->>CloudInit: SSH public keys
    CloudInit->>CloudInit: Substitute ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}
    CloudInit-->>CLI: Processed YAML path

    CLI->>Multipass: multipass launch 24.04 --name devbox --cpus 4 --memory 4G --disk 40G --cloud-init /tmp/processed.yaml
    Multipass-->>CLI: VM created

    CLI->>Multipass: multipass info devbox --format json
    Multipass-->>CLI: {"info":{"devbox":{"ipv4":["192.168.64.5"]}}}

    CLI->>Config: Update config with IP
    CLI-->>User: VM created at 192.168.64.5
```

### VM Creation Sequence (AWS)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant CLI
    participant Config
    participant CloudInit
    participant GitHub
    participant SSM
    participant EC2
    participant CFN
    participant R53

    User->>CLI: goloo create devbox --aws
    CLI->>Config: Load ~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/config.json
    Config-->>CLI: VMConfig + DNSConfig

    CLI->>CloudInit: Process(~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/cloud-init.yaml, users)
    CloudInit->>GitHub: GET /gherlein.keys
    GitHub-->>CloudInit: SSH public keys
    CloudInit-->>CLI: Processed cloud-init content

    CLI->>SSM: GetParameter(/aws/service/canonical/ubuntu/...)
    SSM-->>CLI: ami-0123456789

    CLI->>EC2: DescribeVpcs (find default VPC)
    EC2-->>CLI: vpc-abc123
    CLI->>EC2: DescribeSubnets
    EC2-->>CLI: subnet-def456

    CLI->>CFN: CreateStack(template, params, UserData)
    CFN-->>CLI: StackId

    CLI->>CFN: Wait for CREATE_COMPLETE
    CFN-->>CLI: Success

    CLI->>CFN: DescribeStacks (get outputs)
    CFN-->>CLI: InstanceId, PublicIP, SecurityGroupId

    CLI->>R53: ListHostedZonesByName(domain)
    R53-->>CLI: ZoneId
    CLI->>R53: ChangeResourceRecordSets (A record)
    R53-->>CLI: Success

    CLI->>Config: Update config with outputs
    CLI-->>User: VM created at devbox.example.com (54.1.2.3)
```

### VM Deletion Sequence

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant CLI
    participant Config
    participant Provider
    participant External

    User->>CLI: goloo delete devbox
    CLI->>Config: Load ~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/config.json
    Config-->>CLI: Config (with provider detection)

    alt Multipass (no stack_id)
        CLI->>Provider: Delete via multipass
        Provider->>External: multipass delete devbox
        External-->>Provider: OK
        Provider->>External: multipass purge
        External-->>Provider: OK
    else AWS (has stack_id)
        CLI->>Provider: Delete via AWS
        Provider->>External: Route53 DELETE records
        External-->>Provider: OK
        Provider->>External: CloudFormation DeleteStack
        External-->>Provider: Deletion initiated
        Provider->>External: Wait for DELETE_COMPLETE
        External-->>Provider: OK
        Provider->>External: Delete created VPC/Subnet (if any)
        External-->>Provider: OK
    end

    CLI->>Config: Clear output fields
    CLI-->>User: VM deleted
```

---

## Configuration Schema

### Config File Structure

```mermaid
erDiagram
    Config ||--o| VMConfig : contains
    Config ||--o| DNSConfig : contains
    VMConfig ||--|{ User : has
    VMConfig ||--o{ Mount : has

    Config {
        string provider "optional: multipass or aws"
    }

    VMConfig {
        string name
        int cpus
        string memory
        string disk
        string image
        string instance_type "AWS only"
        string region "AWS only"
        string vpc_id "AWS only, auto-discovered"
        string subnet_id "AWS only, auto-discovered"
        string public_ip "output"
        string instance_id "output"
        string stack_id "output, AWS only"
    }

    User {
        string username
        string github_username
    }

    Mount {
        string source "Multipass only"
        string target "Multipass only"
    }

    DNSConfig {
        string hostname
        string domain
        int ttl
        bool is_apex_domain
        string[] cname_aliases
        string zone_id "output"
        string fqdn "output"
    }
```

### Example Configs

Each VM config lives in a named folder with `config.json` and an optional `cloud-init.yaml`:

```
~/.config/goloo/stacks/devbox/
├── config.json
└── cloud-init.yaml
```

**Local Development (Multipass):**

```json
{
  "vm": {
    "name": "devbox",
    "cpus": 4,
    "memory": "4G",
    "disk": "40G",
    "image": "24.04",
    "users": [
      {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "gherlein"}
    ],
    "mounts": [
      {"source": "/Users/gherlein/src", "target": "/home/ubuntu/src"}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Cloud Development (AWS):**

```json
{
  "vm": {
    "name": "devbox",
    "instance_type": "t3.medium",
    "os": "ubuntu-24.04",
    "region": "us-west-2",
    "users": [
      {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "gherlein"}
    ]
  },
  "dns": {
    "hostname": "devbox",
    "domain": "example.com",
    "ttl": 300
  }
}
```

**Same Config, Both Targets:**

A single config can work for both (DNS ignored for local):

```json
{
  "vm": {
    "name": "devbox",
    "cpus": 4,
    "memory": "4G",
    "disk": "40G",
    "image": "24.04",
    "instance_type": "t3.medium",
    "os": "ubuntu-24.04",
    "region": "us-west-2",
    "users": [
      {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "gherlein"}
    ]
  },
  "dns": {
    "hostname": "devbox",
    "domain": "example.com",
    "ttl": 300
  }
}
```

---

## Provider Interface

```go
package provider

import (
    "context"
    "time"
)

type VMProvider interface {
    Name() string
    Create(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config, cloudInitPath string) error
    Delete(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config) error
    Status(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config) (*VMStatus, error)
    List(ctx context.Context) ([]VMStatus, error)
    SSH(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config) error
    Stop(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config) error
    Start(ctx context.Context, cfg *Config) error
}

type VMStatus struct {
    Name      string
    State     string    // Running, Stopped, etc.
    IP        string
    Provider  string    // multipass, aws
    CreatedAt time.Time
}
```

### Provider Class Diagram

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class VMProvider {
        <<interface>>
        +Name() string
        +Create(ctx, cfg, cloudInitPath) error
        +Delete(ctx, cfg) error
        +Status(ctx, cfg) VMStatus
        +List(ctx) []VMStatus
        +SSH(ctx, cfg) error
        +Stop(ctx, cfg) error
        +Start(ctx, cfg) error
    }

    class MultipassProvider {
        +Name() string
        +Create(ctx, cfg, cloudInitPath) error
        +Delete(ctx, cfg) error
        +Status(ctx, cfg) VMStatus
        +List(ctx) []VMStatus
        +SSH(ctx, cfg) error
        +Stop(ctx, cfg) error
        +Start(ctx, cfg) error
        -runCommand(args) []byte, error
        -parseInfo(json) MultipassInfo
    }

    class AWSProvider {
        -cfnClient CloudFormationClient
        -ec2Client EC2Client
        -r53Client Route53Client
        -ssmClient SSMClient
        +Name() string
        +Create(ctx, cfg, cloudInitPath) error
        +Delete(ctx, cfg) error
        +Status(ctx, cfg) VMStatus
        +List(ctx) []VMStatus
        +SSH(ctx, cfg) error
        +Stop(ctx, cfg) error
        +Start(ctx, cfg) error
        -lookupAMI(os) string
        -discoverVPC() string
        -createNetworkStack() NetworkStack
        -generateCFNTemplate(userData) string
    }

    VMProvider <|.. MultipassProvider
    VMProvider <|.. AWSProvider
```

---

## Cloud-Init Processing

### Variable Substitution Flow

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[~/.config/goloo/stacks/name/cloud-init.yaml] --> B[Read Template]
    B --> C{Has Users?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Fetch GitHub Keys]
    D --> E[github.com/user.keys]
    E --> F[Substitute Variables]
    C -->|No| F
    F --> G[Write Temp File]
    G --> H[Return Path]

    subgraph Variables
        V1["${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}"]
        V2["${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_USERNAME}"]
    end
```

### Supported Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` | GitHub | First user's SSH public keys |
| `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_<USERNAME>}` | GitHub | Specific user's SSH keys (uppercase) |

### Example Cloud-Init

```yaml
#cloud-config
users:
  - name: ubuntu
    groups: sudo
    shell: /bin/bash
    sudo: ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
    ssh_authorized_keys:
      - ${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}

packages:
  - git
  - build-essential
  - golang-go

runcmd:
  - echo "Cloud-init complete" > /var/log/goloo-init.log
```

---

## AWS Implementation Details

### CloudFormation Template

Based on [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2), the generated template includes:

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instance with SSH access

Parameters:
  ImageId:
    Type: String
  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
  VpcId:
    Type: String
  SubnetId:
    Type: String

Resources:
  SSHSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Allow SSH/HTTP/HTTPS
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  EC2Instance:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
      ImageId: !Ref ImageId
      NetworkInterfaces:
        - DeviceIndex: "0"
          SubnetId: !Ref SubnetId
          AssociatePublicIpAddress: true
          GroupSet:
            - !GetAtt SSHSecurityGroup.GroupId
      UserData: <base64-encoded-cloud-init>

Outputs:
  InstanceId:
    Value: !Ref EC2Instance
  PublicIP:
    Value: !GetAtt EC2Instance.PublicIp
  SecurityGroupId:
    Value: !Ref SSHSecurityGroup
```

### AMI Lookup via SSM

```go
var osSSMPaths = map[string]string{
    "ubuntu-24.04": "/aws/service/canonical/ubuntu/server/24.04/stable/current/amd64/hvm/ebs-gp2/ami-id",
    "ubuntu-22.04": "/aws/service/canonical/ubuntu/server/22.04/stable/current/amd64/hvm/ebs-gp2/ami-id",
    "amazon-linux-2023": "/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-x86_64",
}
```

### VPC/Subnet Discovery

1. Try to find default VPC
2. If no default VPC, find any available VPC
3. If no VPC exists, create network stack (VPC + Subnet + IGW + Route Table)
4. Find subnet with public IP auto-assign, or create one

### DNS Record Creation

1. Look up hosted zone ID for domain
2. Create A record: `hostname.domain` → public IP
3. Optionally create CNAME aliases
4. Optionally create apex domain record

---

## CLI Design

### Command Structure

```
goloo <command> <name> [flags]

Commands:
  create    Create a new VM
  delete    Delete a VM
  status    Show VM status
  list      List all VMs
  ssh       SSH into a VM
  stop      Stop a VM
  start     Start a VM

Flags:
  --aws         Use AWS provider (override config)
  --local       Use Multipass provider (override config)
  --dir         Goloo config directory (default: ~/.config/goloo); stacks at <dir>/stacks/
  --folder, -f  Full path to stacks folder (overrides --dir)

Backwards Compatibility (aws-ec2 style):
  -c            Shorthand for create
  -d            Shorthand for delete
  -n            Shorthand for name
```

### Provider Selection Logic

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Parse Flags] --> B{--local flag?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Use Multipass]
    B -->|No| D{--aws flag?}
    D -->|Yes| E[Use AWS]
    D -->|No| F[Load Config]
    F --> G{Has stack_id?}
    G -->|Yes| H[Use AWS - existing stack]
    G -->|No| I{Has dns.domain?}
    I -->|Yes| J[Default: AWS]
    I -->|No| K[Default: Multipass]
```

---

## State Management

### Config File Lifecycle

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Input: User creates config

    Input --> LocalRunning: goloo create (local)
    Input --> AWSRunning: goloo create --aws

    LocalRunning --> LocalRunning: Fields populated
    note right of LocalRunning
        vm.public_ip set
    end note

    AWSRunning --> AWSRunning: Fields populated
    note right of AWSRunning
        vm.instance_id
        vm.public_ip
        vm.stack_id
        dns.zone_id
        dns.fqdn
    end note

    LocalRunning --> LocalStopped: goloo stop
    AWSRunning --> AWSStopped: goloo stop

    LocalStopped --> LocalRunning: goloo start
    AWSStopped --> AWSRunning: goloo start

    LocalRunning --> Input: goloo delete
    LocalStopped --> Input: goloo delete
    AWSRunning --> Input: goloo delete
    AWSStopped --> Input: goloo delete

    Input --> [*]: User deletes file
```

### Output Fields by Provider

| Field | Multipass | AWS |
|-------|-----------|-----|
| `vm.instance_id` | - | EC2 instance ID |
| `vm.public_ip` | Multipass IP | EC2 public IP |
| `vm.stack_id` | - | CloudFormation stack ARN |
| `vm.stack_name` | - | CloudFormation stack name |
| `vm.security_group` | - | Security group ID |
| `vm.ami_id` | - | AMI ID used |
| `vm.vpc_id` | - | VPC ID (discovered/created) |
| `vm.subnet_id` | - | Subnet ID (discovered/created) |
| `dns.zone_id` | - | Route53 zone ID |
| `dns.fqdn` | - | Full DNS name |
| `dns.dns_records` | - | Created DNS records |

---

## Update Strategy: CNAME Swap

For zero-downtime updates, goloo supports blue-green deployment via DNS:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant DNS
    participant Blue as VM (Blue v1.0)
    participant Green as VM (Green v1.1)

    Note over Blue: Running v1.0
    User->>DNS: app.example.com
    DNS->>Blue: 10.0.1.100

    User->>Green: goloo create app-v2 --aws
    Note over Green: Deploy v1.1
    Green->>Green: Cloud-init runs

    User->>Green: Test green VM

    User->>DNS: goloo dns swap app
    Note over DNS: Update A record
    DNS->>DNS: app.example.com → 10.0.2.200

    User->>DNS: app.example.com
    DNS->>Green: 10.0.2.200

    Note over Blue: Keep 1hr for rollback
    User->>Blue: goloo delete app-v1
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
goloo/
├── cmd/goloo/
│   └── main.go              # CLI entry point
├── internal/
│   ├── config/
│   │   ├── config.go        # Config types
│   │   ├── loader.go        # Load, save, validate
│   │   └── loader_test.go
│   ├── cloudinit/
│   │   ├── processor.go     # Variable substitution
│   │   ├── ssh.go           # GitHub key fetching
│   │   └── processor_test.go
│   └── provider/
│       ├── interface.go     # VMProvider interface
│       ├── registry.go      # Provider registry
│       ├── multipass/
│       │   ├── multipass.go # Shell out to multipass
│       │   └── multipass_test.go
│       └── aws/
│           ├── aws.go       # Main provider
│           ├── template.go  # CloudFormation template
│           ├── dns.go       # Route53 operations
│           ├── network.go   # VPC/Subnet discovery
│           └── aws_test.go
├── stacks/                  # example stacks (gitignored); runtime configs live in ~/.config/goloo/stacks/
├── configs/                 # Shared cloud-init templates
│   ├── base.yaml
│   ├── dev.yaml
│   └── claude-dev.yaml
├── Makefile
├── go.mod
├── go.sum
└── README.md
```

---

## Error Handling

### Error Categories

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    E[Error] --> V[Validation Error]
    E --> P[Provider Error]
    E --> N[Network Error]
    E --> C[Config Error]

    V --> V1[Missing required field]
    V --> V2[Invalid username format]
    V --> V3[Cloud-init syntax error]

    P --> P1[Multipass not installed]
    P --> P2[AWS credentials missing]
    P --> P3[Resource already exists]
    P --> P4[Resource not found]
    P --> P5[VPC/Subnet not found]

    N --> N1[GitHub unreachable]
    N --> N2[AWS API timeout]
    N --> N3[CloudFormation failed]

    C --> C1[Config file not found]
    C --> C2[Invalid JSON]
    C --> C3[Unsupported OS]
```

### Error Messages

All errors should be actionable:

```go
var (
    ErrConfigNotFound   = errors.New("config file not found: create ~/.config/goloo/stacks/<name>/config.json")
    ErrProviderUnknown  = errors.New("unknown provider: use 'multipass' or 'aws'")
    ErrVMExists         = errors.New("VM already exists: delete first with 'goloo delete <name>'")
    ErrVMNotFound       = errors.New("VM not found: check 'goloo list' for available VMs")
    ErrNoSSHKeys        = errors.New("no SSH keys found: verify GitHub username at github.com/<user>.keys")
    ErrNoVPC            = errors.New("no VPC found: will create new network infrastructure")
    ErrAWSCredentials   = errors.New("AWS credentials not configured: run 'aws configure'")
    ErrMultipassMissing = errors.New("multipass not installed: visit multipass.run")
)
```

---

## Security Considerations

1. **SSH Keys**: Fetched from GitHub over HTTPS only
2. **AWS Credentials**: Use standard AWS credential chain (env, ~/.aws, IAM role)
3. **Config Files**: May contain sensitive data - gitignored by default
4. **Cloud-Init**: Runs as root - review templates carefully
5. **Security Groups**: SSH open to 0.0.0.0/0 by default - restrict for production
6. **DNS TTL**: Set low (60-300s) to enable quick CNAME swaps

---

## Dependencies

### Go Modules

```go
require (
    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2 v1.x
    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/config v1.x
    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/cloudformation v1.x
    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/ec2 v1.x
    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/route53 v1.x
    github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/ssm v1.x
)
```

### External Dependencies

| Dependency | Required For |
|------------|--------------|
| `multipass` CLI | Local VMs |
| AWS credentials | AWS provider |
| Internet access | GitHub SSH keys, AWS API |

---

## References

- [Multipass Documentation](https://multipass.run/)
- [cloud-init Documentation](https://cloudinit.readthedocs.io/)
- [AWS CloudFormation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cloudformation/)
- [aws-ec2 Repository](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2) - Foundation for AWS provider
- [AWS SDK for Go v2](https://aws.github.io/aws-sdk-go-v2/)

1	# Goloo Development Plan
2	
3	Step-by-step implementation plan for building the unified VM provisioning tool.
4	
5	## Overview
6	
7	Build goloo by:
8	1. Creating the project structure and core types
9	2. Implementing the Multipass provider (shells out to CLI)
10	3. Porting the AWS provider from [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2)
11	4. Unifying with a clean CLI
12	
13	## Prerequisites
14	
15	Before starting:
16	
17	- [ ] Go 1.21+ installed
18	- [ ] Multipass installed and working (`multipass version`)
19	- [ ] AWS credentials configured (`aws sts get-caller-identity`)
20	- [ ] GitHub account (for SSH key testing)
21	
22	---
23	
24	## Phase 1: Project Setup
25	
26	### 1.1 Initialize Repository
27	
28	```bash
29	mkdir goloo && cd goloo
30	go mod init github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo
31	```
32	
33	### 1.2 Create Directory Structure
34	
35	```
36	goloo/
37	├── cmd/goloo/
38	│   └── main.go
39	├── internal/
40	│   ├── config/
41	│   │   ├── config.go
42	│   │   ├── loader.go
43	│   │   └── loader_test.go
44	│   ├── cloudinit/
45	│   │   ├── processor.go
46	│   │   ├── ssh.go
47	│   │   └── processor_test.go
48	│   └── provider/
49	│       ├── interface.go
50	│       ├── registry.go
51	│       ├── multipass/
52	│       │   ├── multipass.go
53	│       │   └── multipass_test.go
54	│       └── aws/
55	│           ├── aws.go
56	│           ├── template.go
57	│           ├── dns.go
58	│           ├── network.go
59	│           └── aws_test.go
60	├── stacks/
61	│   └── .gitkeep
62	├── cloud-init/
63	│   └── (copy from multipass-stuff/configs/)
64	├── Makefile
65	├── go.mod
66	└── README.md
67	```
68	
69	### 1.3 Create Makefile
70	
71	```makefile
72	.PHONY: build run-tests clean install lint
73	
74	BINARY := goloo
75	VERSION := $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
76	LDFLAGS := -ldflags "-X main.version=$(VERSION)"
77	INSTALL_DIR ?= $(HOME)/bin
78	
79	# Default target: show help
80	help:
81		@echo "Usage: make <target>"
82		@echo ""
83		@echo "Targets:"
84		@echo "  build     Build the binary"
85		@echo "  run-tests Run all tests"
86		@echo "  clean     Remove build artifacts"
87		@echo "  install   Install to $(INSTALL_DIR)"
88		@echo "  lint      Run linter"
89	
90	build:
91		go build $(LDFLAGS) -o bin/$(BINARY) ./cmd/goloo
92	
93	run-tests:
94		go test -v ./...
95	
96	clean:
97		rm -rf bin/
98	
99	install: build
100		mkdir -p $(INSTALL_DIR)
101		cp bin/$(BINARY) $(INSTALL_DIR)/
102	
103	lint:
104		go vet ./...
105		@which golangci-lint > /dev/null || echo "Install golangci-lint for more checks"
106	```
107	
108	### 1.4 Acceptance Criteria
109	
110	- [ ] `go build ./...` succeeds
111	- [ ] `go test ./...` runs (even with no tests yet)
112	- [ ] `make build` creates `bin/goloo`
113	- [ ] Directory structure matches plan
114	
115	---
116	
117	## Phase 2: Core Types and Config
118	
119	### 2.1 Define Core Types
120	
121	Create `internal/config/config.go`:
122	
123	```go
124	package config
125	
126	// Config is the top-level configuration
127	type Config struct {
128	    VM  *VMConfig  `json:"vm,omitempty"`
129	    DNS *DNSConfig `json:"dns,omitempty"`
130	}
131	
132	// VMConfig defines VM parameters
133	type VMConfig struct {
134	    // Common fields (both providers)
135	    Name          string  `json:"name"`
136	    CloudInitFile string  `json:"cloud_init_file,omitempty"`
137	    Users         []User  `json:"users,omitempty"`
138	
139	    // Multipass-specific
140	    CPUs    int     `json:"cpus,omitempty"`
141	    Memory  string  `json:"memory,omitempty"`
142	    Disk    string  `json:"disk,omitempty"`
143	    Image   string  `json:"image,omitempty"`
144	    Mounts  []Mount `json:"mounts,omitempty"`
145	
146	    // AWS-specific
147	    InstanceType string `json:"instance_type,omitempty"`
148	    OS           string `json:"os,omitempty"`
149	    Region       string `json:"region,omitempty"`
150	    VpcID        string `json:"vpc_id,omitempty"`
151	    SubnetID     string `json:"subnet_id,omitempty"`
152	
153	    // Output fields (populated after creation)
154	    PublicIP      string `json:"public_ip,omitempty"`
155	    InstanceID    string `json:"instance_id,omitempty"`
156	    StackID       string `json:"stack_id,omitempty"`
157	    StackName     string `json:"stack_name,omitempty"`
158	    SecurityGroup string `json:"security_group,omitempty"`
159	    AMIID         string `json:"ami_id,omitempty"`
160	
161	    // Network tracking (for cleanup)
162	    CreatedVPC            bool   `json:"created_vpc,omitempty"`
163	    CreatedSubnet         bool   `json:"created_subnet,omitempty"`
164	    InternetGatewayID     string `json:"internet_gateway_id,omitempty"`
165	    RouteTableID          string `json:"route_table_id,omitempty"`
166	    RouteTableAssociation string `json:"route_table_association_id,omitempty"`
167	}
168	
169	// DNSConfig defines DNS parameters (AWS only)
170	type DNSConfig struct {
171	    Hostname     string   `json:"hostname,omitempty"`
172	    Domain       string   `json:"domain,omitempty"`
173	    TTL          int      `json:"ttl,omitempty"`
174	    IsApexDomain bool     `json:"is_apex_domain,omitempty"`
175	    CNAMEAliases []string `json:"cname_aliases,omitempty"`
176	
177	    // Output fields
178	    ZoneID     string      `json:"zone_id,omitempty"`
179	    FQDN       string      `json:"fqdn,omitempty"`
180	    DNSRecords []DNSRecord `json:"dns_records,omitempty"`
181	}
182	
183	type User struct {
184	    Username       string `json:"username"`
185	    GitHubUsername string `json:"github_username"`
186	}
187	
188	type Mount struct {
189	    Source string `json:"source"`
190	    Target string `json:"target"`
191	}
192	
193	type DNSRecord struct {
194	    Name  string `json:"name"`
195	    Type  string `json:"type"`
196	    Value string `json:"value"`
197	    TTL   int    `json:"ttl"`
198	}
199	```
200	
201	### 2.2 Implement Config Loader
202	
203	Create `internal/config/loader.go`:
204	
205	```go
206	package config
207	
208	func Load(name string) (*Config, string, error)
209	func Save(path string, cfg *Config) error
210	func ResolvePath(name string) string
211	func ApplyDefaults(cfg *Config)
212	func Validate(cfg *Config) error
213	```
214	
215	Key behaviors:
216	- Check `stacks/<name>.json` first, then treat as path
217	- Apply defaults (cpus=2, memory=2G, disk=20G, region=us-east-1)
218	- Validate required fields (name, at least one user)
219	- Validate username format (lowercase, starts with letter)
220	
221	### 2.3 Write Config Tests
222	
223	Create `internal/config/loader_test.go`:
224	
225	- Test loading valid config
226	- Test path resolution (stacks/ prefix)
227	- Test applying defaults
228	- Test validation errors (missing name, invalid username)
229	- Test saving config updates output fields
230	
231	### 2.4 Acceptance Criteria
232	
233	- [ ] Can load JSON config from stacks/
234	- [ ] Defaults applied for missing fields
235	- [ ] Validation catches missing required fields
236	- [ ] Validation catches invalid username format
237	- [ ] All config tests pass
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Phase 3: Provider Interface
242	
243	### 3.1 Define Interface
244	
245	Create `internal/provider/interface.go`:
246	
247	```go
248	package provider
249	
250	import (
251	    "context"
252	    "time"
253	
254	    "github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo/internal/config"
255	)
256	
257	type VMProvider interface {
258	    Name() string
259	    Create(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, cloudInitPath string) error
260	    Delete(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error
261	    Status(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) (*VMStatus, error)
262	    List(ctx context.Context) ([]VMStatus, error)
263	    SSH(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error
264	    Stop(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error
265	    Start(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error
266	}
267	
268	type VMStatus struct {
269	    Name      string
270	    State     string
271	    IP        string
272	    Provider  string
273	    CreatedAt time.Time
274	}
275	```
276	
277	### 3.2 Create Provider Registry
278	
279	Create `internal/provider/registry.go`:
280	
281	```go
282	package provider
283	
284	var providers = make(map[string]VMProvider)
285	
286	func Register(name string, p VMProvider)
287	func Get(name string) (VMProvider, error)
288	func List() []string
289	```
290	
291	### 3.3 Acceptance Criteria
292	
293	- [ ] Interface compiles
294	- [ ] Registry can register and retrieve providers
295	- [ ] Unknown provider returns clear error
296	
297	---
298	
299	## Phase 4: Cloud-Init Processor
300	
301	### 4.1 Implement SSH Key Fetching
302	
303	Create `internal/cloudinit/ssh.go`:
304	
305	```go

# Goloo Development Plan

Step-by-step implementation plan for building the unified VM provisioning tool.

## Overview

Build goloo by:
1. Creating the project structure and core types
2. Implementing the Multipass provider (shells out to CLI)
3. Porting the AWS provider from [aws-ec2](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/aws-ec2)
4. Unifying with a clean CLI

## Prerequisites

Before starting:

- [ ] Go 1.21+ installed
- [ ] Multipass installed and working (`multipass version`)
- [ ] AWS credentials configured (`aws sts get-caller-identity`)
- [ ] GitHub account (for SSH key testing)

---

## Phase 1: Project Setup

### 1.1 Initialize Repository

```bash
mkdir goloo && cd goloo
go mod init github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo
```

### 1.2 Create Directory Structure

```
goloo/
├── cmd/goloo/
│   └── main.go
├── internal/
│   ├── config/
│   │   ├── config.go
│   │   ├── loader.go
│   │   └── loader_test.go
│   ├── cloudinit/
│   │   ├── processor.go
│   │   ├── ssh.go
│   │   └── processor_test.go
│   └── provider/
│       ├── interface.go
│       ├── registry.go
│       ├── multipass/
│       │   ├── multipass.go
│       │   └── multipass_test.go
│       └── aws/
│           ├── aws.go
│           ├── template.go
│           ├── dns.go
│           ├── network.go
│           └── aws_test.go
├── stacks/
│   └── .gitkeep
├── cloud-init/
│   └── (copy from multipass-stuff/configs/)
├── Makefile
```

```go
func cmdCreate(ctx context.Context, name string, providerName string) error {
    // 1. Load config
    cfg, configPath, err := config.Load(name)

    // 2. Process cloud-init
    cloudInitPath, err := cloudinit.Process(cfg.VM.CloudInitFile, cfg.VM.Users)
    defer os.Remove(cloudInitPath)
```

### 7.4 Provider Selection Logic

```go
func getProvider(ctx context.Context, override string, cfg *config.Config) (provider.VMProvider, error) {
    // 1. Check explicit flag (--aws, --local)
    if override == "aws" { return aws.New(ctx, region) }
    if override == "multipass" { return multipass.New(), nil }

    // 2. Check existing stack (has stack_id = was created with AWS)
    if cfg.VM.StackID != "" { return aws.New(ctx, cfg.VM.Region) }

    // 3. Check DNS config (dns.domain implies AWS)
    if cfg.DNS != nil && cfg.DNS.Domain != "" { return aws.New(ctx, region) }

    // 4. Default to multipass
    return multipass.New(), nil
}
```

## Phase 10: DNS Swap Command

### 10.1 Implement `goloo dns swap`

Add a `dns swap` subcommand for blue-green deployments via DNS. This allows users to deploy a new VM, test it, then atomically switch DNS to point at the new instance.

```bash
goloo dns swap <name>
```

Behavior:
1. Load config for `<name>`
2. Read the current A record for `dns.hostname.dns.domain`
3. Update the A record to point at the new instance's `vm.public_ip`
4. Wait for propagation confirmation
5. Update config with new DNS record values

input.content:
# govirt Requirements ## Overview `govirt` is a unified CLI tool for provisioning VMs locally (libvirt + virt-install + cloud-init) or in the cloud (AWS EC2, DigitalOcean), using a single JSON config file and a standard cloud-init YAML. It replicates all functionality of [goloo](https://github.com/emergingrobotics/goloo) — same commands, same config schema, same cloud-init variable substitution, same cloud provider support — with the local VM backend replaced from Canonical Multipass to libvirt/KVM. --- ## The Problem Same as goloo: you want identical server environments locally and in the cloud, from one config. The difference is that govirt targets systems where libvirt/KVM is the preferred local hypervisor (Linux hosts, servers, CI environments) rather than Multipass. --- ## Solution ```bash govirt create devbox # Local VM via libvirt/KVM govirt create devbox --aws # AWS EC2 instance govirt create devbox --do # DigitalOcean Droplet ``` Same config file, same cloud-init YAML, same commands — only the local backend differs. --- ## Commands ``` govirt create Create a local VM (libvirt) govirt create --aws Create an AWS EC2 instance govirt create --do Create a DigitalOcean Droplet govirt destroy Destroy VM (auto-detects provider) govirt list List local VMs govirt list --aws List AWS VMs govirt list --do List DigitalOcean Droplets govirt ssh SSH into VM govirt status Show VM status govirt stop Stop VM (graceful shutdown) govirt start Start VM govirt dns swap Update DNS records to current VM IP (AWS and DigitalOcean) ``` ### Flags | Flag | Description | |------|-------------| | `--aws` | Use AWS provider | | `--do`, `--digitalocean` | Use DigitalOcean provider | | `--local` | Use local libvirt provider | | `--dir PATH` | govirt config directory (default: `~/.config/govirt`); stacks at `/stacks/` | | `--folder`, `-f PATH` | Full path to stacks folder (overrides `--dir`) | | `--users`, `-u USERS` | GitHub usernames for SSH key injection (comma-separated) | | `--verbose`, `-v` | Show detailed progress | | `--no-hosts` | Skip /etc/hosts management for local VMs | | `--version` | Show version | | `--help`, `-h` | Show help | ### Environment Variables | Variable | Description | |----------|-------------| | `GOVIRT_STACK_FOLDER` | Default base folder for configs (overridden by `--folder`/`-f`) | | `DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN` | DigitalOcean API token (required when using `--do`) | | `GOVIRT_IMAGE_CACHE` | Directory to cache cloud images (default: `~/.cache/govirt/images`) | | `LIBVIRT_DEFAULT_URI` | Libvirt connection URI (default: `qemu:///system`) | --- ## Config File Format Identical to goloo. Each VM config lives in a named folder: ``` ~/.config/govirt/stacks/ └── web-server/ ├── config.json └── cloud-init.yaml ``` ### config.json schema Input sections (user-managed): ```json { "vm": { "name": "web-server", "cpus": 2, "memory": "2G", "disk": "20G", "image": "24.04", "mounts": [ {"source": "/home/user/code", "target": "/home/ubuntu/code"} ], "instance_type": "t3.small", "os": "ubuntu-24.04", "region": "us-east-1", "vpc_id": "", "subnet_id": "", "do_size": "s-2vcpu-4gb", "do_region": "nyc3", "do_image": "ubuntu-24-04-x64", "do_vpc_id": "", "ssh_cidr": "0.0.0.0/0", "web_cidr": "0.0.0.0/0", "users": [ {"username": "ubuntu", "github_username": "alice"}, {"username": "deploy", "github_username": "deploy-bot"} ] }, "dns": { "hostname": "web", "domain": "example.com", "ttl": 300, "zone_id": "", "is_apex_domain": true, "cname_aliases": ["www"] } } ``` State sections (govirt-managed, one per provider): ```json { "local": { "ip": "192.168.122.5", "hosts_entry": true, "domain_name": "web-server", "disk_path": "/var/lib/libvirt/images/web-server.qcow2", "cloud_init_iso": "/var/lib/libvirt/images/web-server-cloud-init.iso", "network": "default" }, "aws": { ... }, "do": { ... } } ``` ### vm section reference | Field | Default | Provider | Description | |-------|---------|----------|-------------| | `name` | (required) | All | VM name | | `cpus` | 2 | Local | Number of vCPUs | | `memory` | `"2G"` | Local | RAM allocation (e.g. `"2G"`, `"512M"`) | | `disk` | `"20G"` | Local | Root disk size | | `image` | `"24.04"` | Local | Ubuntu version shorthand (see Image Mapping) | | `mounts` | | Local | Host directory mounts `{"source", "target"}` | | `instance_type` | `"t3.micro"` | AWS | EC2 instance type | | `os` | `"ubuntu-24.04"` | AWS | AMI lookup key | | `region` | `"us-east-1"` | AWS | AWS region | | `vpc_id` | | AWS | Specific VPC (auto-discovered if empty) | | `subnet_id` | | AWS | Specific subnet (auto-discovered if empty) | | `do_size` | | DigitalOcean | Droplet size slug | | `do_region` | | DigitalOcean | Region slug | | `do_image` | | DigitalOcean | Image slug | | `do_vpc_id` | | DigitalOcean | VPC UUID (optional) | | `ssh_cidr` | `"0.0.0.0/0"` | DigitalOcean | CIDR for SSH firewall rule | | `web_cidr` | `"0.0.0.0/0"` | DigitalOcean | CIDR for HTTP/HTTPS firewall rules | | `users` | (required) | All | List of `{"username", "github_username"}` | --- ## Local VM Backend: libvirt + virt-install + cloud-init This is the primary difference from goloo. All cloud provider functionality (AWS, DigitalOcean) is carried over unchanged. ### Prerequisites (Local) - `libvirt` daemon running (`libvirtd`) - `qemu-kvm` / `qemu-system-x86_64` installed - `virt-install` installed - `virsh` CLI available - `genisoimage` or `cloud-localds` available (for cloud-init ISO generation) - User in `libvirt` group or running with appropriate permissions ### Image Management Govirt downloads Ubuntu cloud images (`.qcow2`) on first use and caches them locally. **Image cache directory**: `~/.cache/govirt/images/` (or `$GOVIRT_IMAGE_CACHE`) **Image mapping** (from `vm.image` shorthand): | `vm.image` | Download URL | |------------|-------------| | `24.04` | `https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/noble/current/noble-server-cloudimg-amd64.img` | | `22.04` | `https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/jammy/current/jammy-server-cloudimg-amd64.img` | | `20.04` | `https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/focal/current/focal-server-cloudimg-amd64.img` | Cached images are never deleted automatically. Users can clear the cache manually. ### Disk Creation For each VM, govirt creates a copy-on-write (CoW) overlay disk backed by the cached cloud image: ``` qemu-img create -f qcow2 -b -F qcow2 ``` The VM disk is stored in the libvirt default storage pool directory (typically `/var/lib/libvirt/images/`) as `.qcow2`. Resizing: `qemu-img resize ` after creation if the size is larger than the base image. ### cloud-init NoCloud ISO Libvirt does not natively inject cloud-init data the way Multipass does. Instead, govirt generates a NoCloud datasource ISO and attaches it as a second CD-ROM drive. The ISO contains two files: - `meta-data`: Instance identity - `user-data`: The processed cloud-init YAML (with SSH keys substituted) **meta-data format:** ```yaml instance-id: govirt- local-hostname: ``` **ISO generation** (one of these commands, whichever is available): ```bash # Option 1: cloud-localds (from cloud-image-utils) cloud-localds # Option 2: genisoimage genisoimage -output -volid cidata -joliet -rock \ user-data meta-data ``` The cloud-init ISO is stored alongside the VM disk: `-cloud-init.iso` After the VM has booted and cloud-init has completed, the ISO is no longer needed but is kept for audit purposes. It is removed when the VM is destroyed. ### VM Creation: virt-install ```bash virt-install \ --name \ --memory \ --vcpus \ --disk path=,format=qcow2 \ --disk path=,device=cdrom \ --os-variant ubuntu24.04 \ --network network=default \ --graphics none \ --noautoconsole \ --import ``` Key flags: - `--import`: Boot from existing disk (the cloud image), no OS install needed - `--noautoconsole`: Do not attach to serial console; return immediately - `--graphics none`: Headless VM - `--network network=default`: Use the libvirt default NAT network (or `--network bridge=
` if configured) **Memory conversion**: `vm.memory` field uses suffix notation (`"2G"`, `"512M"`). Convert to MB for `virt-install --memory`. **OS variant**: Map `vm.image` to `--os-variant` value: | `vm.image` | `--os-variant` | |------------|---------------| | `24.04` | `ubuntu24.04` | | `22.04` | `ubuntu22.04` | | `20.04` | `ubuntu20.04` | If `osinfo-db` does not have the exact variant, fall back to `ubuntu20.04` with a warning. ### Host Directory Mounts (virtiofs) For each `mount` entry in `vm.mounts`: 1. Add to `virt-install` invocation: ```bash --filesystem source=,target=,driver.type=virtiofs ``` where `` is a sanitized version of the target path (replace `/` with `-`, strip leading `-`). 2. The cloud-init `user-data` must include a `runcmd` to mount it: ```yaml runcmd: - mkdir -p - mount -t virtiofs ``` Govirt appends these runcmd entries to the processed cloud-init YAML automatically, based on the `mounts` config — users do not need to add them manually. 3. Add `/etc/fstab` entry via `write_files` in cloud-init for persistence across reboots: ``` virtiofs defaults 0 0 ``` **Fallback to 9p**: If virtiofs is unavailable (host kernel < 5.4 or QEMU < 4.2), fall back to 9p/virtio-9p mounts and emit a warning. ### IP Address Detection After `virt-install` returns, the VM may not have an IP immediately. Govirt polls for the IP using `virsh domifaddr`: ```bash virsh domifaddr --source agent # or if guest agent not available: virsh domifaddr --source lease ``` Poll strategy: 1. Try `virsh domifaddr --source lease` every 2 seconds, up to 60 seconds 2. If no IP after 60 seconds, emit a warning and leave `local.ip` empty 3. Log the IP to `local.ip` once found Alternative: Read DHCP leases from `/var/lib/libvirt/dnsmasq/default.leases` and match by MAC address obtained from `virsh domiflist `. ### cloud-init Completion Detection (verbose mode) In `--verbose` mode, tail the cloud-init log via `virsh console` or `ssh` (once IP is known): ```bash # Via SSH once IP is known: ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ubuntu@ \ "tail -f /var/log/cloud-init-output.log" ``` Poll for SSH availability after IP is detected before attempting to tail the log. ### VM State Operations **Stop (graceful)**: ```bash virsh shutdown ``` Wait up to 30 seconds for the domain to stop. If it doesn't stop, return an error (do not force-kill unless `--force` is added later). **Start**: ```bash virsh start ``` After starting, poll for IP as during creation and update `local.ip`. **Destroy (delete)**: ```bash virsh destroy # force-stop if running virsh undefine --remove-all-storage ``` Also delete the cloud-init ISO file manually (it may not be in the libvirt storage pool). **Status**: ```bash virsh dominfo # state virsh domifaddr # IP ``` Parse `virsh dominfo` XML or text output to get the domain state (running, shut off, paused, etc.). **List**: ```bash virsh list --all ``` Parse output to get name and state. For each running VM, optionally fetch IP via `virsh domifaddr`. ### SSH Direct SSH, not wrapped via `virsh console`: ```bash ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no @ ``` The SSH command replaces the govirt process (exec-style) so the terminal session is seamless. If `local.ip` is not set in state, attempt to look it up via `virsh domifaddr` first. ### /etc/hosts Management Identical to goloo: after local VM creation, add/update `/etc/hosts` entry via `sudo tee`. Remove on destroy. Entry format: ``` # govirt: [.] # /govirt: ``` Marker prefix changes from `goloo:` to `govirt:` to avoid conflicts. ### LibvirtState (local state section) After `govirt create`: ```json { "local": { "ip": "192.168.122.5", "hosts_entry": true, "domain_name": "web-server", "disk_path": "/var/lib/libvirt/images/web-server.qcow2", "cloud_init_iso": "/var/lib/libvirt/images/web-server-cloud-init.iso", "network": "default" } } ``` | Field | Description | |-------|-------------| | `ip` | Last known IP address | | `hosts_entry` | Whether /etc/hosts was updated | | `domain_name` | Libvirt domain name (equals `vm.name`) | | `disk_path` | Absolute path to the VM's qcow2 disk file | | `cloud_init_iso` | Absolute path to the NoCloud cloud-init ISO | | `network` | Libvirt network name used | --- ## Cloud-Init Processing Identical to goloo — no changes. ### Variable Substitution | Variable | Value | |----------|-------| | `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}` | SSH public keys of the first user (fetched from GitHub) | | `${SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_}` | SSH public keys for a specific user (uppercase username) | Keys fetched from `https://github.com/.keys`. Go template syntax (`{{ .Field }}`) is also supported when the cloud-init YAML contains `{{`. ### Mount Injection When `vm.mounts` is non-empty, govirt appends to the processed cloud-init YAML: ```yaml runcmd: - mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/code - mount -t virtiofs home-ubuntu-code /home/ubuntu/code write_files: - path: /etc/fstab append: true content: | home-ubuntu-code /home/ubuntu/code virtiofs defaults 0 0 ``` This injection happens after user variable substitution, before writing the temp file. --- ## Provider Auto-Detection Same logic as goloo, adapted for govirt: 1. If `--local` flag: use libvirt 2. If `--aws` flag: use AWS 3. If `--do` / `--digitalocean` flag: use DigitalOcean 4. Otherwise, check saved state: - Has `do` state section → DigitalOcean - Has `aws` state section → AWS - Default → libvirt --- ## AWS Provider Carried over from goloo verbatim. No changes: - CloudFormation stack management - EC2 instance creation with UserData (cloud-init) - AMI lookup via SSM Parameter Store - VPC/Subnet auto-discovery and creation - Route53 DNS record management - DNS swap (blue-green) --- ## DigitalOcean Provider Carried over from goloo verbatim. No changes: - Droplet creation - Firewall management - DigitalOcean DNS record management - DNS swap --- ## DNS Swap Identical to goloo `dns swap` command. Works for AWS and DigitalOcean. Not applicable to local VMs. --- ## Directory Structure ``` govirt/ ├── cmd/govirt/ │ └── main.go # CLI entry point ├── internal/ │ ├── config/ │ │ ├── config.go # Config types (LocalState extended) │ │ ├── loader.go # Load, save, validate, defaults │ │ └── loader_test.go │ ├── cloudinit/ │ │ ├── processor.go # Variable substitution + mount injection │ │ ├── ssh.go # GitHub key fetching │ │ ├── template.go # Go template rendering │ │ ├── nocloud.go # NoCloud ISO generation (NEW) │ │ └── processor_test.go │ ├── images/ │ │ ├── manager.go # Cloud image download + cache (NEW) │ │ └── manager_test.go │ └── provider/ │ ├── interface.go # VMProvider interface (unchanged) │ ├── libvirt/ # NEW: replaces multipass/ │ │ ├── libvirt.go # Shell out to virsh + virt-install │ │ ├── virsh.go # virsh command wrappers │ │ ├── iso.go # cloud-init ISO generation │ │ ├── network.go # IP detection, network queries │ │ └── libvirt_test.go │ ├── aws/ # Unchanged from goloo │ │ ├── aws.go │ │ ├── clients.go │ │ ├── dns.go │ │ ├── network.go │ │ ├── sdk_*.go │ │ └── template.go │ └── digitalocean/ # Unchanged from goloo │ ├── digitalocean.go │ ├── client.go │ ├── dns.go │ ├── droplet.go │ └── firewall.go ├── configs/ # Shared cloud-init templates (same as goloo) │ ├── base.yaml │ ├── dev.yaml │ ├── python-dev.yaml │ ├── go-dev.yaml │ ├── node-dev.yaml │ └── claude-dev.yaml ├── examples/ # Example stacks (same as goloo) │ ├── web-server/ │ ├── dev-server/ │ ├── aws-web-server/ │ └── do-server/ ├── Makefile ├── go.mod └── README.md ``` --- ## Go Module ``` module github.com/gherlein/govirt go 1.21 ``` Dependencies (same as goloo plus libvirt tooling via CLI shelling): - `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2` (AWS provider — unchanged) - `github.com/digitalocean/godo` (DigitalOcean provider — unchanged) - `golang.org/x/oauth2` (DigitalOcean auth — unchanged) - No Go libvirt bindings needed: govirt shells out to `virsh` and `virt-install` CLI tools, mirroring how goloo shells out to `multipass`. --- ## Config Schema Changes from goloo The `Config` struct gains a richer `LocalState`: ```go type LocalState struct { IP string `json:"ip,omitempty"` HostsEntry bool `json:"hosts_entry,omitempty"` DomainName string `json:"domain_name,omitempty"` DiskPath string `json:"disk_path,omitempty"` CloudInitISO string `json:"cloud_init_iso,omitempty"` Network string `json:"network,omitempty"` } ``` Everything else in `Config`, `VMConfig`, `DNSConfig`, `AWSState`, `DOState`, `User`, `Mount`, `DNSRecord` is identical to goloo. --- ## Provider Interface Identical to goloo `VMProvider` interface — no changes: ```go type VMProvider interface { Name() string Create(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, cloudInitPath string) error Delete(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error Status(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) (*VMStatus, error) List(ctx context.Context) ([]VMStatus, error) SSH(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error Stop(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error Start(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config) error } ``` The libvirt provider satisfies this interface and is registered as `"libvirt"` internally, but exposed to users as the default local provider (no `--libvirt` flag needed; `--local` selects it). --- ## LibvirtProvider: Create Sequence 1. Validate `vm.name`, `vm.users`, `vm.cpus`, `vm.memory`, `vm.disk`, `vm.image` 2. Resolve and download cloud image if not cached (`internal/images`) 3. Create CoW qcow2 overlay disk from cached image 4. Resize disk to `vm.disk` if larger than base image 5. Generate cloud-init NoCloud ISO (meta-data + processed user-data) - meta-data: `instance-id: govirt-`, `local-hostname: ` - user-data: the `cloudInitPath` argument (already processed by caller) 6. If `vm.mounts` is non-empty, inject mount runcmd into a temporary modified cloud-init file and regenerate the ISO 7. Build and execute `virt-install` command 8. Poll for IP via `virsh domifaddr` (up to 60s, every 2s) 9. Populate `cfg.Local` with all state fields 10. Return (caller saves state) --- ## LibvirtProvider: Delete Sequence 1. Force-stop domain if running: `virsh destroy ` (ignore errors if already stopped) 2. Undefine domain and remove storage: `virsh undefine --remove-all-storage` 3. Remove cloud-init ISO: `os.Remove(cfg.Local.CloudInitISO)` 4. Set `cfg.Local = nil` 5. Return --- ## Error Messages All errors actionable, following goloo patterns: ```go var ( ErrLibvirtMissing = errors.New("virsh not found: install libvirt (apt install libvirt-daemon-system)") ErrVirtInstallMissing = errors.New("virt-install not found: install virtinst (apt install virtinst)") ErrGenISOmissing = errors.New("neither cloud-localds nor genisoimage found: install cloud-image-utils or genisoimage") ErrImageDownloadFailed = errors.New("failed to download cloud image: check network and retry") ErrNoIP = errors.New("VM started but no IP assigned within 60s: check libvirt network with 'virsh net-list'") ErrDomainExists = errors.New("VM already exists: destroy first with 'govirt destroy '") ErrDomainNotFound = errors.New("VM not found: check 'govirt list' for available VMs") ErrKVMUnavailable = errors.New("KVM not available: check /dev/kvm exists and current user is in 'kvm' group") ) ``` --- ## Prerequisites Check On `govirt create` (local), check before proceeding: 1. `/dev/kvm` exists (KVM available) 2. `virsh` is in PATH 3. `virt-install` is in PATH 4. At least one of `cloud-localds` or `genisoimage` is in PATH 5. Current user can connect to `qemu:///system` (or `LIBVIRT_DEFAULT_URI`) If any check fails, print a clear error with the install command. --- ## Defaults | Field | Default | |-------|---------| | `vm.cpus` | `2` | | `vm.memory` | `"2G"` | | `vm.disk` | `"20G"` | | `vm.image` | `"24.04"` | | `vm.region` | `"us-east-1"` | | `vm.instance_type` | `"t3.micro"` | | `dns.ttl` | `300` | | libvirt network | `"default"` | | image cache dir | `~/.cache/govirt/images` | | stack folder | `~/.config/govirt/stacks` | --- ## Simultaneous Deployments Identical to goloo: separate `local`, `aws`, and `do` state sections allow one config to have active deployments on multiple providers simultaneously: ```json { "vm": { ... }, "local": { "ip": "192.168.122.5", "domain_name": "web-server", ... }, "aws": { "public_ip": "54.1.2.3", ... }, "do": { "public_ip": "192.241.0.1", ... } } ``` Delete each independently: ```bash govirt destroy web-server --local govirt destroy web-server --aws govirt destroy web-server --do ``` --- ## cloud-init Template Library Same templates as goloo (copy verbatim): | Config | What it installs | |--------|-----------------| | `configs/base.yaml` | curl, wget, vim, htop, git | | `configs/dev.yaml` | build-essential, ripgrep, tmux, fzf, jq | | `configs/python-dev.yaml` | Python 3, pip, venv, uv | | `configs/go-dev.yaml` | Go 1.23, gopls, delve debugger | | `configs/node-dev.yaml` | Node.js 22, npm, pnpm, yarn | | `configs/claude-dev.yaml` | All of the above plus Docker | --- ## Security Considerations 1. SSH keys fetched from GitHub over HTTPS only 2. AWS credentials use standard AWS credential chain 3. `config.json` files may contain sensitive state — gitignore by default 4. Cloud-init ISO contains SSH public keys — store with mode 0600 5. VM disk files: follow libvirt storage pool permissions (typically `root:libvirt-qemu`) 6. `sudo tee` used for `/etc/hosts` management — same as goloo 7. DigitalOcean token never written to disk or logged --- ## Testing Requirements ### Unit Tests - Config loading, defaults, validation (`internal/config`) - Cloud-init variable substitution (`internal/cloudinit`) - Mount injection into cloud-init YAML (`internal/cloudinit`) - NoCloud ISO meta-data generation (`internal/cloudinit`) - Image URL resolution from shorthand (`internal/images`) - `virt-install` command argument building (`internal/provider/libvirt`) - `virsh domifaddr` output parsing (`internal/provider/libvirt`) - `virsh list` output parsing (`internal/provider/libvirt`) - `virsh dominfo` state parsing (`internal/provider/libvirt`) ### Integration Tests (require libvirt) Manual test matrix: | Test | libvirt | AWS | DigitalOcean | |------|---------|-----|--------------| | Create with cloud-init | | | | | SSH into VM | | | | | Stop VM | | | | | Start VM | | | | | Status check | | | | | List VMs | | | | | Destroy VM | | | | | Host dir mount | | N/A | N/A | | DNS creation | N/A | | | | DNS deletion | N/A | | | | DNS swap | N/A | | | --- ## Implementation Phases ### Phase 1: Project Setup - Initialize `github.com/gherlein/govirt` Go module - Copy directory structure from goloo - Update module paths - Stub out `internal/provider/libvirt/` package - Verify `go build ./...` succeeds ### Phase 2: Config Types - Copy `internal/config/config.go` from goloo - Extend `LocalState` with libvirt fields (`DomainName`, `DiskPath`, `CloudInitISO`, `Network`) - Copy `internal/config/loader.go` from goloo - Add `~/.cache/govirt/images` default to loader - Update tests ### Phase 3: Cloud-Init Processor - Copy `internal/cloudinit/processor.go`, `ssh.go`, `template.go` from goloo - Add `internal/cloudinit/nocloud.go`: generate NoCloud meta-data content - Add mount injection to processor (append virtiofs runcmd + fstab write_files) - Update tests ### Phase 4: Image Manager - Implement `internal/images/manager.go`: - Map image shorthand to download URL - Download with progress reporting - Verify SHA256 if checksum file is available - Cache in `$GOVIRT_IMAGE_CACHE` - Return path to cached image ### Phase 5: LibvirtProvider - Implement `internal/provider/libvirt/`: - `virsh.go`: wrappers for `virsh` commands (list, dominfo, domifaddr, destroy, undefine, start, shutdown) - `iso.go`: generate NoCloud cloud-init ISO via `cloud-localds` or `genisoimage` - `network.go`: IP detection via `virsh domifaddr`, DHCP lease fallback - `libvirt.go`: main `Provider` struct implementing `VMProvider` interface - Unit tests for command building and output parsing ### Phase 6: CLI - Copy `cmd/goloo/main.go` from goloo - Replace all `multipass.New()` with `libvirt.New()` - Update provider names ("multipass" → "libvirt") - Update binary name to `govirt` - Update stack folder default from `~/.config/goloo` to `~/.config/govirt` - Update environment variable prefix from `GOLOO_` to `GOVIRT_` - Update /etc/hosts marker from `goloo:` to `govirt:` - Update help text and error messages ### Phase 7: Cloud Providers - Copy `internal/provider/aws/` from goloo verbatim (no changes) - Copy `internal/provider/digitalocean/` from goloo verbatim (no changes) - Wire into `getProvider()` in CLI ### Phase 8: Integration and Polish - End-to-end tests against real libvirt - Error message review - Prerequisite checks - README --- ## Key Differences from goloo (Summary) | Aspect | goloo | govirt | |--------|-------|--------| | Local backend | Multipass | libvirt + virt-install + KVM | | Local VM tool | `multipass launch` | `virt-install --import` | | cloud-init delivery | Multipass native | NoCloud ISO (second disk) | | Image management | Multipass auto-downloads | govirt downloads qcow2 to `~/.cache/govirt/images` | | Host mounts | `multipass mount` | virtiofs (or 9p fallback) | | SSH to local VM | `multipass shell ` | `ssh ubuntu@` | | IP detection | `multipass info --format json` | `virsh domifaddr` | | Stop/Start | `multipass stop/start` | `virsh shutdown` / `virsh start` | | List | `multipass list --format json` | `virsh list --all` | | Delete | `multipass delete && purge` | `virsh destroy && undefine --remove-all-storage` | | State fields | `local.ip` | `local.ip` + `local.domain_name` + `local.disk_path` + `local.cloud_init_iso` + `local.network` | | Config dir | `~/.config/goloo` | `~/.config/govirt` | | Env var prefix | `GOLOO_` | `GOVIRT_` | | /etc/hosts marker | `# goloo:` | `# govirt:` | | AWS provider | unchanged | unchanged | | DigitalOcean provider | unchanged | unchanged | | cloud-init processing | unchanged | unchanged + mount injection | | Config schema | `LocalState{IP, HostsEntry}` | `LocalState{IP, HostsEntry, DomainName, DiskPath, CloudInitISO, Network}` |
